questions.explanation: Grandmother Willow is a wise, spiritual tree who guides Pocahontas.

questions.question: What is the name of the greedy English governor who leads the settlers?
questions.explanation: Governor Ratcliffe leads the English settlers and searches obsessively for gold.

questions.question: Who is Pocahontas's father, the chief of her tribe?
questions.explanation: Chief Powhatan leads his people and is Pocahontas's father.

questions.question: In which song does Pocahontas describe the beauty and spirit of nature to John Smith?
questions.explanation: Pocahontas sings 'Colors of the Wind' to teach John Smith about respecting nature.

questions.question: What does Mulan disguise herself as in order to take her father's place in the army?
questions.explanation: Mulan cuts her hair and dresses as a man to secretly join the army in her father's place.

questions.question: What small, talkative dragon becomes Mulan's guardian companion?
questions.explanation: Mushu is the tiny dragon who accompanies Mulan and tries to help her succeed.

questions.question: In which country does the story of 'Mulan' take place?
questions.explanation: Mulan is set in ancient China, where she joins the army to defend her homeland.

questions.question: Who leads the invading army that threatens China in the film?
questions.explanation: Shan Yu commands the Huns who invade China, prompting the Emperor's call to arms.

questions.question: What is the name of Mulan's father, the aging soldier she wants to protect?
questions.explanation: Fa Zhou is Mulan's father, whom she secretly replaces in the army to spare him from battle.

questions.question: What false name does Mulan use while disguised as a soldier?
questions.explanation: Mulan adopts the name 'Ping' to hide her identity while training as a soldier.

questions.question: Which song plays while Mulan and the other recruits train to become soldiers?
answers: I'll Make a Man Out of You
questions.explanation: 'I'll Make a Man Out of You' is sung by Captain Li Shang during the recruits' training montage.

questions.question: What is Tiana's biggest dream while working hard in New Orleans?
questions.explanation: Tiana dreams of opening her own restaurant, inspired by her late father's ambition.

questions.question: What kind of animal is Louis, who dreams of playing jazz trumpet?
questions.explanation: Louis is a friendly alligator who loves music and dreams of performing jazz.

questions.question: What is the name of the lovestruck firefly who helps Tiana and Naveen?
questions.explanation: Ray the Cajun firefly befriends Tiana and Naveen and guides them through the bayou.

questions.question: Who is the sinister voodoo man who turns Prince Naveen into a frog?
questions.explanation: Dr. Facilier uses dark magic to transform Prince Naveen into a frog.

questions.question: What is the name of Tiana's hardworking father, who shared her love of cooking?
questions.explanation: James is Tiana's father, whose dream of owning a restaurant inspires her own.

questions.question: In which American city is 'The Princess and the Frog' set?
questions.explanation: The film takes place in and around New Orleans during the 1920s.

questions.question: What happens to Tiana right after she kisses the frog Prince Naveen?
answers: She turns into a frog too
questions.explanation: Instead of breaking the spell, Tiana's kiss causes her to become a frog as well.

questions.question: What is unique about Rapunzel's incredibly long hair?
answers: It changes color with her mood
It can turn into rope instantly
It glows and has healing magic
questions.explanation: Rapunzel's magical golden hair glows and can heal wounds or reverse aging when she sings.

questions.question: What kind of animal is Pascal, Rapunzel's loyal companion?
questions.explanation: Pascal is a color-changing chameleon who stays by Rapunzel's side throughout her tower years.

questions.question: What is the name of the persistent palace horse who chases Flynn Rider?
questions.explanation: Maximus is the loyal, determined horse who pursues Flynn Rider before joining the adventure.

questions.question: Who kidnaps baby Rapunzel and raises her in a hidden tower?
questions.explanation: Mother Gothel steals Rapunzel as a baby to keep her magical hair for herself.

questions.question: What is the name of the kingdom that Rapunzel is the lost princess of?
questions.explanation: Rapunzel is revealed to be the long-lost princess of the kingdom of Corona.

questions.question: What household item does Rapunzel skillfully use as a weapon throughout the film?
questions.explanation: Rapunzel wields a heavy frying pan to defend herself throughout her journey.

questions.question: What yearly event do Rapunzel and Flynn watch together, filled with floating lights?
questions.explanation: Rapunzel and Flynn watch thousands of floating lanterns released during the kingdom's yearly festival.

questions.question: In which song do Rapunzel and Flynn watch the floating lanterns from a boat?
questions.explanation: Rapunzel and Flynn share a tender moment during the song 'I See the Light.'

questions.question: What skill is Merida especially talented at, as shown in an archery contest?
questions.explanation: Merida is a highly skilled archer who competes in the contest for her own hand in marriage.

questions.question: What is the name of Merida's loyal horse?
questions.explanation: Angus is Merida's clever and loyal Clydesdale horse.

questions.question: Who is Merida's father, the boisterous king of DunBroch?
questions.explanation: King Fergus is Merida's loud, good-natured father and ruler of DunBroch.

questions.question: What happens to Queen Elinor after Merida gives her an enchanted cake?
answers: She falls into a deep sleep
questions.explanation: A witch's spell hidden in the cake accidentally transforms Queen Elinor into a bear.

questions.question: What is the name of the Scottish kingdom ruled by Merida's family?
questions.explanation: DunBroch is the Scottish kingdom ruled by King Fergus and Queen Elinor.

questions.question: Which song plays as Merida rides freely through the highlands on Angus?
questions.explanation: 'Touch the Sky' plays as Merida rides through the Scottish highlands, celebrating her freedom.

questions.question: What role is Moana destined to inherit within her village?
questions.explanation: As the chief's daughter, Moana is destined to one day lead her people as chief.

questions.question: What kind of animal is Pua, Moana's pet who stays behind on the island?
questions.explanation: Pua is Moana's loyal pet pig who remains on Motunui during her voyage.

questions.question: What demigod does Moana recruit to help restore the Heart of Te Fiti?
questions.explanation: Moana seeks out the demigod Maui, who once stole the Heart of Te Fiti.

questions.question: What must Moana do to save her island from a mysterious blight?
answers: Build a new fleet of canoes
Sail beyond the reef to restore the heart
questions.explanation: Moana sails beyond her island's reef to find Maui and restore the stolen Heart of Te Fiti.

questions.question: Who is Moana's grandmother, who encourages her to sail beyond the reef?
questions.explanation: Gramma Tala inspires Moana to follow the call of the ocean and voyage beyond the reef.

questions.question: Which brave princess is a skilled archer in the film Brave?
questions.explanation: Merida is a talented archer who defies tradition in the Scottish Highlands.

questions.question: What glowing object must Moana return to stop the islands from dying?
questions.explanation: Moana must return the stolen Heart of Te Fiti to restore life to the islands.

questions.question: In which song does Moana express her longing to sail across the ocean?
questions.explanation: Moana sings 'How Far I'll Go' about her deep desire to explore the ocean.

questions.question: What magical power does Princess Elsa possess?
answers: The power to create ice and snow
The power to talk to animals
questions.explanation: Elsa was born with the magical power to create ice and snow.

questions.question: What is the name of Elsa's younger sister, who sets off to find her?
questions.explanation: Princess Anna journeys to find her sister Elsa after she flees Arendelle.

questions.question: What is the name of the magical snowman brought to life by Elsa's powers?
questions.explanation: Olaf is the cheerful snowman that Elsa's magic brings to life.

questions.question: What kind of animal is Sven, Kristoff's loyal companion?
questions.explanation: Sven is Kristoff's faithful pet reindeer who travels everywhere with him.

questions.question: Which prince betrays Anna and reveals he never truly loved her?
questions.explanation: Prince Hans deceives Anna, revealing his true plan to seize the throne of Arendelle.

questions.question: Who are the parents of princesses Anna and Elsa?
questions.explanation: King Agnarr and Queen Iduna are the parents of Anna and Elsa.

questions.question: What is the name of the kingdom ruled by Anna and Elsa's family?
questions.explanation: Arendelle is the Scandinavian-inspired kingdom ruled by Anna and Elsa's family.

questions.question: What accidentally happens to the kingdom after Elsa's powers are revealed at her coronation?
answers: It is frozen in an eternal winter
questions.explanation: Elsa accidentally plunges Arendelle into an eternal winter when her icy powers are exposed.

questions.question: In which song does Elsa embrace her powers and leave her old life behind?
answers: Do You Want to Build a Snowman
For the First Time in Forever
questions.explanation: Elsa sings 'Let It Go' as she embraces her powers and builds her ice palace.

questions.question: In Disney's Cinderella (1950), what color is the gown Cinderella wears to the royal ball?
questions.explanation: Cinderella arrives at the ball in a shimmering silvery-blue gown transformed by her Fairy Godmother.

questions.question: In the animated Beauty and the Beast, what color is Belle's famous ballgown?
questions.explanation: Belle wears an iconic golden-yellow gown during the ballroom dance with the Beast.

questions.question: In The Little Mermaid, what color is Ariel's mermaid tail?
questions.explanation: Ariel is known for her bright green mermaid tail and purple seashell top.

questions.question: In Tangled, what color is Rapunzel's magical long hair?
questions.explanation: Rapunzel's seventy feet of magical hair is golden blonde and glows when she sings.

questions.question: Which Disney princess belongs to the Powhatan people in colonial Virginia?
questions.explanation: Pocahontas is the daughter of Chief Powhatan and a member of the Powhatan people.

questions.question: In Frozen, what royal title does Elsa receive at her coronation?
questions.explanation: Elsa is crowned Queen of Arendelle at the coronation ceremony after coming of age.

questions.question: Which princess sails beyond the reef to save her home island of Motunui?
questions.explanation: Moana voyages across the ocean to restore the heart and save her island of Motunui.

questions.question: In Aladdin, which princess falls in love with a street boy named Aladdin?
questions.explanation: Princess Jasmine of Agrabah falls in love with Aladdin despite his humble background.

questions.question: In Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs, what color is Snow White's hair?
questions.explanation: Snow White has hair as black as ebony, matching the description in the fairy tale.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: Which princess works as a hardworking waitress in 1920s New Orleans?
questions.explanation: Tiana waits tables while saving to open her own restaurant in The Princess and the Frog.

questions.question: In Sleeping Beauty, the good fairies argue over making Aurora's dress which two colors?
questions.explanation: Flora wants Aurora's dress pink while Merryweather insists on blue, changing it repeatedly.

questions.question: In Raya and the Last Dragon, Raya is a warrior princess searching for the last what?
questions.explanation: Raya searches for Sisu, the last dragon, hoping to defeat the Druun and restore Kumandra.

questions.question: In Aladdin, what kind of animal is Rajah, Jasmine's protective pet?
questions.explanation: Rajah is Jasmine's loyal pet tiger who guards her within the palace of Agrabah.

questions.question: In the animated Mulan, what kind of lucky creature is Cri-Kee?
questions.explanation: Cri-Kee is a cricket believed to bring good luck who accompanies Mulan and Mushu.

questions.question: In Pocahontas, what kind of bird is Flit, Pocahontas's feisty companion?
questions.explanation: Flit is a protective ruby-throated hummingbird who travels alongside Pocahontas.

questions.question: In The Little Mermaid, what kind of bird is Scuttle, who advises Ariel about human objects?
questions.explanation: Scuttle is a seagull who gives Ariel comically wrong names for human items.

questions.question: In Moana, what kind of animal is the clumsy Heihei who ends up on Moana's boat?
questions.explanation: Heihei is a dim-witted rooster who accidentally becomes Moana's voyaging companion.

questions.question: In Disney's Cinderella, what is the name of Cinderella's faithful dog?
questions.explanation: Bruno is Cinderella's loyal bloodhound who dislikes the wicked cat Lucifer.

questions.question: In Beauty and the Beast, what is the name of Belle's horse?
questions.explanation: Philippe is Belle's dependable horse who leads her to the Beast's castle.

questions.question: In Beauty and the Beast, which enchanted servant has been turned into a talking mantel clock?
questions.explanation: Cogsworth, the head of household, is enchanted into a fussy mantel clock.

questions.question: In the animated Mulan, what is the name of the family horse Mulan rides to war?
questions.explanation: Khan is Mulan's strong black horse who carries her while she serves in the army.

questions.question: In Aladdin, what kind of bird is Iago, Jafar's sarcastic sidekick?
questions.explanation: Iago is a loud, scheming parrot who assists the villain Jafar.

questions.question: In Disney's Cinderella, what is the name of Lady Tremaine's sly pet cat?
questions.explanation: Lucifer is the spoiled cat who torments the mice and Cinderella's dog Bruno.

questions.question: In Raya and the Last Dragon, what is the name of the last dragon who helps Raya?
questions.explanation: Sisu is the good-hearted water dragon who joins Raya on her quest.

questions.question: In Pocahontas, what kind of pampered pet does Governor Ratcliffe keep, named Percy?
questions.explanation: Percy is Ratcliffe's spoiled pug who later befriends Meeko the raccoon.

questions.question: In Disney's Cinderella, what is the name of Cinderella's cruel stepmother?
questions.explanation: Lady Tremaine is the cold stepmother who forces Cinderella to serve the household.

questions.question: In Snow White, the Evil Queen disguises herself as what to deliver the poisoned apple?
questions.explanation: The Evil Queen drinks a potion to become a haggard old peddler before tricking Snow White.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: In Sleeping Beauty, Maleficent transforms into what fearsome creature in the final battle?
questions.explanation: Maleficent turns into a towering black-and-purple dragon to fight Prince Phillip.

questions.question: In The Little Mermaid, what are the names of Ursula's two eel minions?
questions.explanation: Flotsam and Jetsam are the glowing-eyed eels who serve the sea witch Ursula.

questions.question: In Beauty and the Beast, what is the name of Gaston's bumbling sidekick?
questions.explanation: LeFou is Gaston's short, devoted follower who cheers on his every boast.

questions.question: In Frozen, Prince Hans comes from which place?
questions.explanation: Hans is the thirteenth prince of the Southern Isles who secretly schemes for a throne.

questions.question: In Tangled, why does Mother Gothel keep Rapunzel locked away in the tower?
answers: To use her hair to stay young
questions.explanation: Gothel hoards Rapunzel because her magical hair keeps Gothel youthful when she sings.

questions.question: In The Princess and the Frog, by what nickname is the villain Dr. Facilier known?
questions.explanation: Dr. Facilier is called the Shadow Man for his dark magic and shadowy helpers.

questions.question: In Pocahontas, what does the greedy Governor Ratcliffe believe he will find in the New World?
questions.explanation: Ratcliffe is obsessed with finding gold and orders the settlers to dig for it.

questions.question: In Brave, what is the name of the legendary demon bear?
questions.explanation: Mor'du is the monstrous bear, once a prince, whose curse warns of Elinor's fate.

questions.question: In Frozen, the greedy visiting Duke who wants to exploit Arendelle comes from which town?
questions.explanation: The Duke of Weselton hopes to profit from Arendelle and turns on Elsa in fear.

questions.question: In Beauty and the Beast, Gaston rallies the villagers to storm the castle and kill whom?
questions.explanation: Gaston leads an angry mob to the castle intending to kill the Beast out of jealousy.

questions.question: In Brave, what is the name of Merida's mother?
questions.explanation: Queen Elinor is Merida's dignified mother who is accidentally turned into a bear.

questions.question: In Brave, how many mischievous little brothers does Merida have?
questions.explanation: Merida has three troublemaking triplet brothers: Harris, Hubert, and Hamish.

questions.question: In Moana, what is the name of Moana's father, the chief of Motunui?
questions.explanation: Chief Tui is Moana's protective father who wants her to stay safely on the island.

questions.question: In The Princess and the Frog, Prince Naveen hails from which faraway kingdom?
questions.explanation: Prince Naveen comes from the fictional kingdom of Maldonia before visiting New Orleans.

questions.question: In Tangled, who are Rapunzel's real parents?
answers: The King and Queen of Corona
questions.explanation: Rapunzel is the lost daughter of the King and Queen of Corona, stolen as a baby.

questions.question: In Sleeping Beauty, what is the name of Princess Aurora's mother?
questions.explanation: Queen Leah is Aurora's mother and the wife of King Stefan.

questions.question: In Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs, how is the Evil Queen related to Snow White?
questions.explanation: The vain Evil Queen is Snow White's jealous stepmother.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: In Aladdin, what does the law require Princess Jasmine to do before her next birthday?
questions.explanation: By law Jasmine must marry a prince before her birthday, a rule she resents.

questions.question: In Disney's Cinderella, what happened to Cinderella's father, leaving her with her stepmother?
questions.explanation: Cinderella's father dies, after which Lady Tremaine treats her as a servant.

questions.question: In Frozen, what happens to Anna and Elsa's parents while the girls are young?
questions.explanation: The King and Queen of Arendelle are lost in a storm at sea, leaving the sisters alone.

questions.question: In Sleeping Beauty, the three good fairies raise Aurora by pretending to be her what?
questions.explanation: Flora, Fauna, and Merryweather pose as Aurora's aunts while hiding her in the forest.

questions.question: In Raya and the Last Dragon, what is the name of Raya's father, chief of the Heart land?
questions.explanation: Chief Benja is Raya's father, who dreams of reuniting the divided regions of Kumandra.

questions.question: In the animated Beauty and the Beast, in which country is Belle's village located?
questions.explanation: Belle lives in a quiet provincial town in France.

questions.question: In Moana, what is the name of Moana's home island?
questions.explanation: Motunui is the Polynesian island where Moana is destined to become chief.

questions.question: In Pocahontas, the English settlers arrive to build a colony in which land?
questions.explanation: The settlers sail to the land of Virginia, home of Pocahontas's Powhatan people.

questions.question: In Aladdin, the Cave of Wonders lies in the desert outside which city?
questions.explanation: The Cave of Wonders holding the magic lamp sits in the desert beyond Agrabah.

questions.question: The kingdom of Arendelle in Frozen is inspired by the landscapes of which country?
questions.explanation: Arendelle's fjords and architecture draw inspiration from Norway.

questions.question: In Moana, what is the name of the mother island goddess whose heart is restored?
questions.explanation: Te Fiti is the island goddess whose stolen heart Moana journeys to return.

questions.question: In Raya and the Last Dragon, what is the once-united land divided into five regions?
questions.explanation: Kumandra was one prosperous land before splitting into Heart, Fang, Talon, Spine, and Tail.

questions.question: In Brave, the kingdom of DunBroch is set in the Highlands of which country?
questions.explanation: Brave is set in the rugged Scottish Highlands, home to Merida's kingdom of DunBroch.

questions.question: In Sleeping Beauty, where do the fairies raise Aurora in hiding?
answers: In a cottage in the forest
questions.explanation: The fairies raise Aurora in a woodcutter's cottage deep in the forest to hide her.

questions.question: In Aladdin, Aladdin poses as 'Prince Ali' from the made-up kingdom of what?
questions.explanation: The Genie invents the kingdom of Ababwa so Aladdin can pose as royal Prince Ali.

questions.question: In Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs, what kind of mine do the seven dwarfs work in?
questions.explanation: The dwarfs mine diamonds and other jewels each day, singing 'Heigh-Ho' on the way home.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: In The Princess and the Frog, as frogs Tiana and Naveen end up lost in the Louisiana what?
questions.explanation: Turned into frogs, Tiana and Naveen are stranded in the Louisiana bayou.

questions.question: In Aladdin, what magical object flies Aladdin and Jasmine around on their adventures?
questions.explanation: The sentient Magic Carpet from the Cave of Wonders carries Aladdin and Jasmine aloft.

questions.question: In The Little Mermaid, what powerful three-pronged weapon does King Triton wield?
questions.explanation: King Triton commands the sea with his magical golden trident.

questions.question: In Moana, what magical object lets Maui shapeshift into different animals?
questions.explanation: Maui's giant magical fishhook gives him the power to transform into creatures.

questions.question: In Tangled, the magical golden flower's healing power was first used to heal whom?
questions.explanation: The golden flower healed Corona's ailing queen, passing its magic to baby Rapunzel's hair.

questions.question: In Brave, what magical blue lights lead Merida through the forest?
questions.explanation: Glowing will-o'-the-wisps guide Merida toward her fate throughout the film.

questions.question: In Sleeping Beauty, the fairies give Prince Phillip the Shield of Virtue and which weapon?
questions.explanation: Phillip receives the Sword of Truth and Shield of Virtue to defeat Maleficent.

questions.question: In Disney's Cinderella, what does the Fairy Godmother use to perform her magic?
questions.explanation: The Fairy Godmother waves her magic wand while singing 'Bibbidi-Bobbidi-Boo.'

questions.question: In Pocahontas, what treasured keepsake from her late mother does Pocahontas wear?
questions.explanation: Pocahontas wears her late mother's necklace, a meaningful gift from her father.

questions.question: In the animated Beauty and the Beast, the magical rose is kept protected under a glass what?
questions.explanation: The enchanted rose floats beneath a glass dome, its petals counting down the Beast's time.

questions.question: In Disney's Cinderella, which item stays magically intact even after midnight?
questions.explanation: The glass slippers remain after midnight, letting the Prince identify Cinderella.

questions.question: In Tangled, what happens to Rapunzel's hair once it is cut?
answers: It loses its magic and turns brown
questions.explanation: When cut, Rapunzel's hair loses its golden glow and healing magic, turning brown.

questions.question: In Beauty and the Beast, what finally breaks the Beast's curse and restores his human form?
answers: Belle telling him she loves him
questions.explanation: Belle's declaration of love breaks the enchantment and turns the Beast back into a prince.

questions.question: In Sleeping Beauty, how is Princess Aurora awakened from her deep sleep?
questions.explanation: True love's kiss from Prince Phillip breaks Maleficent's curse and wakes Aurora.

questions.question: In Aladdin, what does Aladdin use his final wish for?
questions.explanation: Aladdin keeps his promise and uses his third wish to grant the Genie freedom.

questions.question: In Pocahontas, how does Pocahontas save John Smith from her father?
questions.explanation: Pocahontas throws herself over John Smith to stop his execution and plead for peace.

questions.question: In the animated Mulan, how does Mulan defeat most of the Hun army in the mountains?
questions.explanation: Mulan aims a cannon at the mountain to cause an avalanche that buries the Hun army.

questions.question: In Frozen, what thaws Anna's frozen heart and saves her?
answers: Her own act of true love
questions.explanation: Anna's own act of true love, sacrificing herself to save Elsa, thaws her frozen heart.

questions.question: In Brave, how does Merida break the spell and turn her mother back?
questions.explanation: Merida mends the tapestry she tore, repairing the bond and lifting Elinor's bear curse.

questions.question: In Tangled, what saves Eugene after Rapunzel's magical hair is cut?
questions.explanation: A single tear from Rapunzel still carries the flower's magic and heals Eugene.

questions.question: In The Little Mermaid, how does Ariel get to remain human at the end?
questions.explanation: King Triton uses his trident to transform Ariel into a human so she can marry Eric.

questions.question: In Snow White, what do the dwarfs do after Snow White falls into her deep sleep?
answers: Place her in a glass coffin
questions.explanation: Unable to bury her, the dwarfs lay Snow White in a glass-and-gold coffin.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: In The Princess and the Frog, what finally turns Tiana and Naveen back into humans?
questions.explanation: Marriage makes Tiana a princess, so their kiss finally breaks the frog curse.

questions.question: In Moana, what does Moana realize about the lava monster Te Kā?
answers: It is Te Fiti without her heart
questions.explanation: Moana realizes Te Kā is Te Fiti transformed by rage after her heart was stolen.

questions.question: In Disney's Cinderella, how is Cinderella finally identified as the mystery girl?
questions.explanation: Cinderella is identified when the glass slipper fits her foot perfectly.

questions.question: In The Little Mermaid, which song does Sebastian sing about how wonderful life is underwater?
questions.explanation: Sebastian sings 'Under the Sea' to convince Ariel that ocean life is better than land.

questions.question: In the animated Mulan, which song does Mulan sing about not recognizing herself?
questions.explanation: Mulan sings 'Reflection,' wondering when her mirror will show who she truly is.

questions.question: In Aladdin, which song does the Genie sing to show off his magical powers?
questions.explanation: The Genie performs 'Friend Like Me' to introduce his dazzling wish-granting powers.

questions.question: In Beauty and the Beast, which tavern song boasts about how great Gaston is?
questions.explanation: LeFou leads the song 'Gaston' in the tavern to cheer up the boastful hunter.

questions.question: In Moana, which song does Maui sing bragging about his heroic deeds?
questions.explanation: Maui sings 'You're Welcome,' listing the gifts he claims to have given humankind.

questions.question: In Tangled, which song describes Rapunzel's daily chores inside her tower?
questions.explanation: 'When Will My Life Begin' shows Rapunzel's routine of cleaning, reading, and painting.

questions.question: In Frozen, in which song does young Anna repeatedly ask Elsa to come play?
answers: For the First Time in Forever
Do You Want to Build a Snowman?
questions.explanation: 'Do You Want to Build a Snowman?' follows Anna knocking on Elsa's door over the years.

questions.question: In Pocahontas, which song expresses Pocahontas's longing for what lies ahead in life?
questions.explanation: In 'Just Around the Riverbend,' Pocahontas wonders what unknown path her future holds.

questions.question: In The Princess and the Frog, which song does Tiana sing about her restaurant dream?
questions.explanation: Tiana sings 'Almost There,' vowing that hard work will make her dream come true.

questions.question: In Snow White, which song does Snow White sing while tidying the dwarfs' cottage?
questions.explanation: Snow White sings 'Whistle While You Work' as the woodland animals help her clean.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: In Disney's Cinderella, which song does Cinderella sing about keeping faith in her dreams?
answers: A Dream Is a Wish Your Heart Makes
questions.explanation: Cinderella sings 'A Dream Is a Wish Your Heart Makes' about never giving up hope.

questions.question: In Beauty and the Beast, the opening song introduces townsfolk gossiping about which girl?
questions.explanation: The opening number 'Belle' shows villagers describing Belle as beautiful but peculiar.

questions.question: In The Little Mermaid, which song does Ursula sing while making her deal with Ariel?
questions.explanation: Ursula sings 'Poor Unfortunate Souls' as she persuades Ariel to trade her voice.

questions.question: In Frozen, what warm season does the snowman Olaf dream about experiencing?
questions.explanation: In Frozen, the cheerful snowman Olaf sings 'In Summer' about his dream of experiencing summertime.

questions.question: In The Little Mermaid, what is the name of the human prince Ariel falls in love with?
questions.explanation: Ariel rescues and falls in love with Prince Eric after saving him from a shipwreck.

questions.question: Which of the Seven Dwarfs serves as the leader of the group in Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs?
questions.explanation: Doc is portrayed as the well-meaning but fumbling leader of the Seven Dwarfs.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: What disguise does the Evil Queen wear to trick Snow White into eating the poisoned apple?
questions.explanation: The Evil Queen transforms herself into an old hag selling apples to deceive Snow White.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: What magical object does the Evil Queen ask to reveal who is the fairest in the land?
questions.explanation: The Evil Queen consults her Magic Mirror, which always answers her truthfully.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: What is the Evil Queen's relationship to Snow White in the original Disney film?
questions.explanation: The Evil Queen is Snow White's jealous stepmother, envious of her beauty.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: What breaks the sleeping death spell placed on Snow White by the poisoned apple?
questions.explanation: A kiss of true love from the Prince awakens Snow White from her enchanted sleep.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: Which song do the Seven Dwarfs sing while marching home from the mine?
questions.explanation: "Heigh-Ho" is the cheerful marching tune the dwarfs sing on their way home from work.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: Which of the Seven Dwarfs is known for his uncontrollable sneezing?
questions.explanation: Sneezy is the dwarf whose powerful sneezes often cause chaos among the group.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: What is the name of the plump, kind-hearted mouse who befriends Cinderella?
questions.explanation: Gus, also called Gus-Gus, is the roly-poly mouse who becomes one of Cinderella's dearest friends.

questions.question: What kind of animal is Pascal, Rapunzel's companion in Tangled?
questions.explanation: Pascal is Rapunzel's loyal pet chameleon who changes color with his mood.

questions.question: In Sleeping Beauty, what are the names of the three good fairies?
questions.explanation: Flora, Fauna, and Merryweather raise Aurora in hiding to protect her from Maleficent.

questions.question: What object does the Fairy Godmother turn into Cinderella's carriage?
questions.explanation: The Fairy Godmother transforms an ordinary pumpkin into an elegant carriage for the ball.

questions.question: By what time must Cinderella return home before the Fairy Godmother's magic disappears?
questions.explanation: The Fairy Godmother warns Cinderella that all her magic will end at the stroke of midnight.

questions.question: What is the name of Cinderella's loyal dog?
questions.explanation: Bruno is Cinderella's devoted dog who often chases after Lucifer the cat.

questions.question: What song does Cinderella sing to express her hopes despite her hard life at home?
answers: A Dream Is a Wish Your Heart Makes
questions.explanation: "A Dream Is a Wish Your Heart Makes" reflects Cinderella's enduring hope for a better future.

questions.question: What are the names of the three good fairies who raise Aurora in hiding?
questions.explanation: Flora, Fauna, and Merryweather raise Aurora in a woodland cottage to protect her from Maleficent.

questions.question: According to Maleficent's curse, what will cause Princess Aurora to fall into a deathlike sleep?
answers: Pricking her finger on a spindle
questions.explanation: Maleficent curses Aurora to prick her finger on a spinning wheel's spindle before sundown on her sixteenth birthday.

questions.question: What two magical gifts do the fairies give Prince Phillip to help him defeat Maleficent?
answers: A cloak of invisibility and a golden key
An enchanted bow and silver arrows
The Shield of Virtue and the Sword of Truth
A crystal shield and a fire sword
questions.explanation: The three good fairies arm Prince Phillip with the Shield of Virtue and the Sword of Truth to battle Maleficent.

questions.question: What is the name of Aurora's father, the king, in Sleeping Beauty?
questions.explanation: King Stefan is Aurora's father, who hosts the celebration where Maleficent delivers her curse.

questions.question: What ultimately breaks the sleeping curse placed on Princess Aurora?
answers: A magic potion from the fairies
questions.explanation: True love's kiss from Prince Phillip awakens Aurora from her enchanted sleep.

questions.question: What name do the three fairies give Aurora while secretly raising her in the forest?
questions.explanation: The fairies raise Aurora under the alias "Briar Rose" to hide her from Maleficent.

questions.question: In Sleeping Beauty, by what name do the fairies raise Aurora in the forest?
questions.explanation: To hide her from Maleficent, the fairies call Aurora by the name Briar Rose.

questions.question: What is the name of the enchanted candelabra who befriends Belle in the Beast's castle?
questions.explanation: Lumiere is the charming candelabra servant who welcomes Belle to the Beast's enchanted castle.

questions.question: What is the name of the young teacup, son of Mrs. Potts, in Beauty and the Beast?
questions.explanation: Chip is the cheerful little teacup and youngest member of the enchanted household.

questions.question: Who is the vain hunter determined to marry Belle in Beauty and the Beast?
questions.explanation: Gaston is the boastful, self-obsessed hunter who pursues Belle throughout the film.

questions.question: What is the name of Belle's father, the eccentric inventor?
questions.explanation: Maurice is Belle's loving father, whose invention leads him to the Beast's castle.

questions.question: What magical object marks the deadline for the Beast to break his curse?
questions.explanation: The Beast must fall in love before the last petal falls from the enchanted rose.

questions.question: How does Belle end up a prisoner in the Beast's castle?
answers: She loses a bet with the Beast
She offers to take her father's place
questions.explanation: Belle offers herself as prisoner in exchange for her father Maurice's freedom.

questions.question: What song opens Beauty and the Beast and introduces Belle's provincial town?
questions.explanation: "Belle" is the opening number that introduces Belle and the townspeople who find her odd.

questions.question: What is the name of Ariel's loyal, timid fish friend?
questions.explanation: Flounder is the small, nervous fish who accompanies Ariel on many of her adventures.

questions.question: Which crab serves as court composer and reluctant chaperone to Ariel?
questions.explanation: Sebastian is King Triton's court composer, tasked with keeping an eye on Ariel.

questions.question: What is the name of the sea witch who offers Ariel a deal to become human?
questions.explanation: Ursula, the sea witch, trades Ariel's voice for a temporary pair of human legs.

questions.question: Who is Ariel's father, ruler of the underwater kingdom?
questions.explanation: King Triton is Ariel's father and the powerful ruler of Atlantica.

questions.question: What is the name of the underwater kingdom where Ariel's merfolk family lives?
questions.explanation: Atlantica is the undersea kingdom ruled by King Triton and home to Ariel.

questions.question: Ursula traps Ariel's stolen voice inside what object worn around her neck?
questions.explanation: Ursula stores Ariel's voice inside a nautilus shell that she wears as a necklace.

questions.question: Which song is Ariel's signature ballad about longing to live among humans?
questions.explanation: "Part of Your World" expresses Ariel's deep desire to explore life on land.

questions.question: In Aladdin, what is the name of the tiger-shaped cave holding the magic lamp?
questions.explanation: The Cave of Wonders is a talking, tiger-shaped cavern guarding the magic lamp.

questions.question: Who is the power-hungry royal vizier and main villain of Aladdin?
questions.explanation: Jafar is the scheming royal vizier who seeks the Genie's lamp to gain ultimate power.

questions.question: What title does Jasmine's father hold as ruler of Agrabah?
questions.explanation: Jasmine's father rules Agrabah as the Sultan, wishing to see his daughter happily married.

questions.question: What is the name of the fictional kingdom where Aladdin and Jasmine live?
questions.explanation: Agrabah is the fictional desert kingdom that serves as the setting for Aladdin.

questions.question: What magical object, when rubbed, releases the Genie in Aladdin?
questions.explanation: The Genie is bound to an ordinary-looking oil lamp until someone rubs it free.

questions.question: What alias does Aladdin use when he arrives at the palace to court Jasmine?
questions.explanation: Aladdin disguises himself as the wealthy "Prince Ali" using the Genie's magic.

questions.question: What song do Aladdin and Jasmine sing while flying on the magic carpet?
questions.explanation: "A Whole New World" is the romantic duet Aladdin and Jasmine share during their carpet ride.

questions.question: In Pocahontas, which ancient talking willow tree gives Pocahontas advice?
questions.explanation: Grandmother Willow is the wise spirit tree who guides Pocahontas.

questions.question: What kind of animal is Flit, one of Pocahontas's loyal companions?
questions.explanation: Flit is the small, protective hummingbird who often frets over Pocahontas's safety.

questions.question: Who is the greedy governor leading the English settlers in Pocahontas?
questions.explanation: Governor Ratcliffe leads the English expedition, driven by his obsession with finding gold.

questions.question: What is the name of Pocahontas's father, the tribal chief?
questions.explanation: Chief Powhatan is Pocahontas's father and the respected leader of their people.

questions.question: How does Pocahontas save John Smith from execution?
answers: She shields him with her own body
She helps him escape at night
She challenges her father to a duel
questions.explanation: Pocahontas throws herself over John Smith to stop her father from executing him.

questions.question: Which song is Pocahontas's signature number about her connection to nature?
questions.explanation: "Colors of the Wind" reflects Pocahontas's philosophy about nature and interconnected life.

questions.question: What is the name of the small dragon who becomes Mulan's guardian and comic sidekick?
questions.explanation: Mushu is the tiny, talkative dragon sent to protect Mulan on her journey.

questions.question: What kind of creature is Cri-Kee, Mulan's supposed good-luck charm?
questions.explanation: Cri-Kee is the lucky cricket that Grandmother Fa gives Mulan for good fortune.

questions.question: Who is the ruthless leader of the Hun army in Mulan?
questions.explanation: Shan Yu commands the invading Hun army that threatens Imperial China.

questions.question: In the animated Mulan, who is the Hun leader threatening China?
questions.explanation: Shan Yu is the ruthless commander of the invading Hun army.

questions.question: What iconic structure is shown being breached by Shan Yu's Hun army in the opening scene of Mulan?
questions.explanation: The film opens with Shan Yu and the Huns scaling the Great Wall of China to begin their invasion.

questions.question: In the animated Mulan, what lucky insect travels with Mulan?
questions.explanation: Cri-Kee is the lucky cricket sent along to watch over Mulan.

questions.question: Which song reflects Mulan's inner struggle over her identity before joining the army?
answers: I'll Make a Man Out of You
questions.explanation: "Reflection" captures Mulan's conflict between her true self and society's expectations.

questions.question: What is the name of the jazz-loving alligator who befriends Tiana?
questions.explanation: Louis is the trumpet-playing alligator who dreams of performing jazz with humans.

questions.question: What is the name of the Cajun firefly who guides Tiana and Naveen through the bayou?
questions.explanation: Ray is the good-natured firefly who helps Tiana and Naveen navigate the swamp.

questions.question: Who is the voodoo sorcerer known as the 'Shadow Man' in The Princess and the Frog?
questions.explanation: Dr. Facilier is the sinister voodoo sorcerer who tricks Naveen and sets the plot in motion.

questions.question: What is the name of Tiana's father, whose restaurant dream inspires her own?
questions.explanation: Tiana's father James dreamed of opening a restaurant, a dream Tiana works to fulfill.

questions.question: Prince Naveen hails from what fictional kingdom in The Princess and the Frog?
questions.explanation: Prince Naveen is a visiting prince from the fictional kingdom of Maldonia.

questions.question: What happens when Tiana kisses the frog Prince Naveen, hoping to break his curse?
answers: She turns into a frog too
She gains the ability to speak to animals
questions.explanation: Since Tiana isn't actually a princess at that point, kissing Naveen turns her into a frog as well.

questions.question: Which song expresses Tiana's determination to open her own restaurant?
questions.explanation: "Almost There" showcases Tiana's drive and ambition to achieve her restaurant dream.

questions.question: What is the name of Rapunzel's loyal pet chameleon?
questions.explanation: Pascal is Rapunzel's color-changing chameleon and closest companion in her tower.

questions.question: What is the name of the palace horse who becomes an ally to Flynn Rider and Rapunzel?
questions.explanation: Maximus is the determined palace guard horse who ends up helping Rapunzel and Flynn.

questions.question: Who kidnaps Rapunzel as a baby to keep her magical hair for herself?
questions.explanation: Mother Gothel steals baby Rapunzel to selfishly use her hair's youth-restoring magic.

questions.question: What magical flower's power is passed into Rapunzel's hair before she is born?
questions.explanation: A magical golden flower that healed the pregnant Queen passes its power into Rapunzel's hair.

questions.question: Rapunzel is revealed to be the long-lost princess of which kingdom?
questions.explanation: Rapunzel is the missing princess of Corona, stolen from the castle as an infant.

questions.question: What do Rapunzel's parents release into the sky every year on her birthday?
questions.explanation: The King and Queen release floating lanterns each year, hoping their lost daughter will return.

questions.question: What song do Rapunzel and Flynn Rider sing while watching the floating lanterns?
questions.explanation: "I See the Light" is the romantic duet Rapunzel and Flynn share during the lantern scene.

questions.question: What is the name of Merida's loyal horse in Brave?
questions.explanation: Angus is Merida's sturdy Clydesdale horse who carries her through the Highlands.

questions.question: What is the name of the demon bear that was once a cursed prince in Brave?
questions.explanation: Mor'du is a legendary demon bear, once a prince transformed by seeking a selfish spell.

questions.question: What are the names of Merida's mischievous triplet younger brothers?
questions.explanation: Merida's triplet brothers, Harris, Hubert, and Hamish, are constantly causing mischief.

questions.question: Merida is the princess of which Scottish kingdom in Brave?
questions.explanation: Merida is the princess of the Scottish Highland kingdom of DunBroch.

questions.question: What magical item from the witch's cottage accidentally turns Queen Elinor into a bear?
questions.explanation: Merida obtains an enchanted cake from a witch, intending to change her mother's mind, but it turns her into a bear instead.

questions.question: What unexpected transformation happens to Queen Elinor after eating the witch's spell cake?
questions.explanation: Queen Elinor is transformed into a bear, forcing Merida to find a way to reverse the spell.

questions.question: Which song plays as Merida rides freely through the Highlands early in Brave?
questions.explanation: "Touch the Sky" underscores Merida's love of freedom as she rides across the Highlands.

questions.question: What is the name of the not-too-bright rooster who accidentally stows away on Moana's boat?
questions.explanation: Heihei is the clueless rooster who ends up joining Moana's voyage by accident.

questions.question: Which demigod joins Moana on her voyage to restore the heart of Te Fiti?
questions.explanation: Maui, a shapeshifting demigod, reluctantly joins Moana on her ocean voyage.

questions.question: What is the name of the lava monster Moana confronts, later revealed to be a transformed goddess?
questions.explanation: Te Kā is the fiery lava demon that Moana discovers is actually Te Fiti without her heart.

questions.question: What is the name of Moana's father, chief of their island village?
questions.explanation: Chief Tui is Moana's father, who leads their people and initially forbids sailing beyond the reef.

questions.question: In Moana, which shape-shifting demigod joins Moana on her voyage?
questions.explanation: Maui, a boastful demigod, sails with Moana to restore the heart of Te Fiti.

questions.question: What magical stone must Moana return to stop a growing blight on the islands?
questions.explanation: Moana must return the Heart of Te Fiti, a small green stone, to save the islands from darkness.

questions.question: Which song is Moana's signature ballad about her longing for the open ocean?
questions.explanation: "How Far I'll Go" expresses Moana's deep pull toward the sea and adventure beyond her island.

questions.question: What is the name of the friendly snowman brought to life by Elsa's magic?
questions.explanation: Olaf is the cheerful snowman created by Elsa's powers, who dreams of experiencing summer.

questions.question: What is the name of Kristoff's loyal pet reindeer in Frozen?
questions.explanation: Sven is Kristoff's devoted reindeer companion who travels with him throughout the film.

questions.question: Which prince pretends to love Anna but ultimately betrays her to seize the throne?
questions.explanation: Prince Hans of the Southern Isles deceives Anna, revealing his true plan to claim Arendelle's throne.

questions.question: What are the names of Anna and Elsa's parents, the King and Queen of Arendelle?
questions.explanation: Anna and Elsa's parents are King Agnarr and Queen Iduna, rulers of Arendelle.

questions.question: What is the name of the kingdom ruled by Elsa in Frozen?
questions.explanation: Arendelle is the Scandinavian-inspired kingdom that Elsa rules as queen.

questions.question: What must happen to save Anna after Elsa accidentally freezes her heart?
answers: A magic spell from the trolls
A journey to the North Mountain
questions.explanation: Only an act of true love can save Anna after her heart is struck by Elsa's magic.

questions.question: Which song does Elsa sing after fleeing to the North Mountain and embracing her powers?
answers: For the First Time in Forever
Do You Want to Build a Snowman?
questions.explanation: "Let It Go" is Elsa's powerful anthem as she embraces her magic and isolates herself on the mountain.

questions.question: How long is Rapunzel's magical golden hair in Tangled?
questions.explanation: Disney states Rapunzel's enchanted, glowing hair measures 70 feet long throughout Tangled.

questions.question: In The Princess and the Frog, what job does Tiana work to save for her restaurant?
questions.explanation: Tiana works long shifts as a waitress in New Orleans to save money for her dream restaurant.

questions.question: In Brave, which weapon is Princess Merida especially skilled with?
questions.explanation: Merida is a gifted archer, famously splitting her suitors' targets with her bow and arrow.

questions.question: In the animated Mulan, what is Mulan's family surname?
questions.explanation: In Disney's animated Mulan, the heroine is Fa Mulan, daughter of Fa Zhou.

questions.question: In Beauty and the Beast, what pastime is Belle best known for enjoying?
questions.explanation: Belle loves reading, often walking through her provincial town with a book in hand.

questions.question: What chore is Snow White doing when she is first introduced in the film?
questions.explanation: Snow White is shown scrubbing the castle courtyard steps in rags when the film opens.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: In The Little Mermaid, what does Ariel collect and hide in her secret grotto?
questions.explanation: Ariel fills her grotto with human treasures like forks and other curious objects from the surface.

questions.question: In the animated Mulan, what does Mulan cut with a sword before leaving for the army?
questions.explanation: Mulan slices off her long hair with her father's sword to disguise herself as a male soldier.

questions.question: In Moana, who or what chooses Moana to carry the heart of Te Fiti?
questions.explanation: The sentient ocean selects young Moana and gives her the heart of Te Fiti to restore.

questions.question: In Tangled, what does Rapunzel use as a weapon when she first meets Flynn Rider?
questions.explanation: Rapunzel knocks out the intruder Flynn Rider by swinging her trusty frying pan.

questions.question: In the live-action Beauty and the Beast (2017), what device does Belle invent?
questions.explanation: In the 2017 film, Belle rigs a barrel contraption to wash laundry so she can read.

questions.question: In Pocahontas, what kind of animal is her mischievous companion Meeko?
questions.explanation: Meeko is a food-loving raccoon who accompanies Pocahontas throughout the film.

questions.question: In Aladdin, what kind of animal is Aladdin's loyal companion Abu?
questions.explanation: Abu is Aladdin's pet monkey and frequent partner in his street escapades in Agrabah.

questions.question: In Aladdin, what is the name of Jafar's scheming parrot sidekick?
questions.explanation: Iago is Jafar's sarcastic parrot who helps carry out the vizier's plots in Agrabah.

questions.question: In Aladdin, what is the name of Princess Jasmine's pet tiger?
questions.explanation: Rajah is Jasmine's protective pet tiger who lives with her in the palace.

questions.question: Which of the Seven Dwarfs never speaks throughout Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs?
questions.explanation: Dopey is the only dwarf who never talks, communicating through gestures and expressions instead.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: In Cinderella, what is the name of the slim, quick mouse who is Gus's best friend?
questions.explanation: Jaq is the thin, clever mouse who, with roly-poly Gus, helps Cinderella around the house.

questions.question: In Cinderella, what is the name of the stepmother's spoiled pet cat?
questions.explanation: Lucifer is Lady Tremaine's mean-spirited cat who torments Cinderella's mouse friends.

questions.question: In Sleeping Beauty, what is the name of Prince Phillip's faithful horse?
questions.explanation: Samson is Prince Phillip's loyal steed who carries him to King Stefan's castle to wake Aurora.

questions.question: In Moana, what kind of animal is Moana's pet Pua?
questions.explanation: Pua is Moana's affectionate pet pig who lives with her on the island of Motunui.

questions.question: In Cinderella, what is the name of the cruel stepmother?
questions.explanation: Lady Tremaine is Cinderella's cold, controlling stepmother who favors her own daughters.

questions.question: In Cinderella, what are the names of the two jealous stepsisters?
questions.explanation: Anastasia and Drizella are Cinderella's vain, unkind stepsisters, daughters of Lady Tremaine.

questions.question: In Sleeping Beauty, what creature does Maleficent transform into for the final battle?
questions.explanation: Maleficent turns into a massive black-and-purple dragon to fight Prince Phillip.

questions.question: In Sleeping Beauty, what is the name of Maleficent's pet raven?
questions.explanation: Diablo is Maleficent's loyal raven who searches the kingdom to find the hidden Aurora.

questions.question: In Snow White, what does the Evil Queen order the Huntsman to bring back as proof?
questions.explanation: The Queen demands Snow White's heart in a box as proof of her death, but the Huntsman spares her.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: In Aladdin, what does Jafar wish to become, which becomes his undoing?
questions.explanation: Jafar wishes to become an all-powerful genie, but is trapped inside a lamp by its rules.

questions.question: In the animated Mulan, what kind of bird accompanies the villain Shan Yu?
questions.explanation: Shan Yu, the Hun leader, is accompanied by a fierce falcon that scouts ahead for his army.

questions.question: In Sleeping Beauty, where does Maleficent keep her lair and hold Prince Phillip captive?
questions.explanation: Maleficent's ruined castle sits atop the Forbidden Mountain, where she imprisons Prince Phillip.

questions.question: In The Little Mermaid, what human name does Ursula take when disguised to marry Eric?
questions.explanation: Ursula disguises herself as a dark-haired beauty named Vanessa, using Ariel's stolen voice.

questions.question: In Frozen, which sister is older, Anna or Elsa?
questions.explanation: Elsa is the elder sister and heir to the throne of Arendelle, with Anna being younger.

questions.question: In The Little Mermaid, how many daughters does King Triton have?
questions.explanation: King Triton has seven mermaid daughters, of whom Ariel is the youngest.

questions.question: In the animated Mulan, what is the name of Mulan's father?
questions.explanation: Fa Zhou is Mulan's aging father, whose army conscription Mulan secretly takes in his place.

questions.question: In Brave, what is the name of Merida's father, the king of DunBroch?
questions.explanation: King Fergus is Merida's boisterous father, a warrior who lost his leg to the bear Mor'du.

questions.question: In Aladdin, how many children does the Sultan have?
questions.explanation: Princess Jasmine is the Sultan of Agrabah's only child and heir.

questions.question: In Moana, what is the name of Moana's wise grandmother?
questions.explanation: Gramma Tala encourages Moana to follow the ocean's call and embrace her people's voyaging past.

questions.question: In The Princess and the Frog, what is the name of Tiana's mother?
questions.explanation: Eudora is Tiana's mother, a talented seamstress who sews dresses for the wealthy La Bouff family.

questions.question: In Cinderella, why does Cinderella end up under her stepmother's control?
answers: She was orphaned as a baby
questions.explanation: After Cinderella's father dies, her stepmother mistreats her and forces her into servitude.

questions.question: In Sleeping Beauty, to which prince is baby Aurora betrothed?
questions.explanation: Aurora is betrothed as an infant to Prince Phillip to unite the two neighboring kingdoms.

questions.question: In the live-action Beauty and the Beast (2017), what happened to Belle's mother?
questions.explanation: The 2017 film reveals Belle's mother died of the plague in Paris when Belle was a baby.

questions.question: In Pocahontas, which warrior does Chief Powhatan wish Pocahontas to marry?
questions.explanation: Chief Powhatan wants his daughter to marry Kocoum, a serious and brave Powhatan warrior.

questions.question: In Moana, what is the name of the island goddess whose heart must be restored?
questions.explanation: Te Fiti is the mother island goddess whose stolen heart Moana journeys to return.

questions.question: In Brave, which is one of the clans whose sons compete for Merida's hand?
questions.explanation: The clans Macintosh, MacGuffin, and Dingwall each present a son to compete for Merida.

questions.question: In which country is Beauty and the Beast set?
questions.explanation: Belle's story unfolds in a French provincial town, reflecting the tale's French origins.

questions.question: The Sultan's palace in Aladdin was famously inspired by which real building?
questions.explanation: The domed Agrabah palace design was inspired by India's white-marble Taj Mahal.

questions.question: In Pocahontas, which colony do the English settlers sail to establish?
questions.explanation: The settlers aboard the Susan Constant travel to found the Jamestown colony in Virginia.

questions.question: In which country is the animated Mulan set?
questions.explanation: Mulan is set in Imperial China, where Mulan defends her homeland from a Hun invasion.

questions.question: In Tangled, the kingdom of Corona is depicted as what kind of location?
questions.explanation: Corona is shown as an island city connected by a bridge, surrounded by water.

questions.question: In Cinderella, by what name is the king's son commonly known?
questions.explanation: The royal heir Cinderella dances with at the ball is known as Prince Charming.

questions.question: In Aladdin, what enchanted object carries Aladdin and Jasmine on their rides?
questions.explanation: The living Magic Carpet, found in the Cave of Wonders, flies Aladdin and Jasmine across Agrabah.

questions.question: In Tangled, what happens when Rapunzel sings the healing incantation to her hair?
questions.explanation: Rapunzel's hair glows golden and heals wounds or restores youth when she sings its incantation.

questions.question: In Tangled, what color does Rapunzel's hair turn once it is cut?
questions.explanation: When cut, Rapunzel's magical golden hair turns brown and permanently loses its powers.

questions.question: In Cinderella, the Fairy Godmother transforms the mice into what?
questions.explanation: The Fairy Godmother turns Cinderella's mouse friends into white horses to pull the carriage.

questions.question: In Sleeping Beauty, what does Merryweather's gift change Maleficent's death curse into?
questions.explanation: Merryweather softens the curse so Aurora will only fall into a deep sleep, broken by true love's kiss.

questions.question: In Aladdin, how many wishes does the Genie grant his master?
questions.explanation: The Genie grants exactly three wishes, with the rules that he cannot kill, revive, or force love.

questions.question: In Beauty and the Beast, what enchanted object is the character Cogsworth?
questions.explanation: Cogsworth, the Beast's uptight majordomo, was transformed into a mantel clock by the curse.

questions.question: In Beauty and the Beast, what enchanted object lets Belle see faraway people and places?
questions.explanation: The Beast gives Belle a magic mirror that shows anything she wishes to see, including her ailing father.

questions.question: In Frozen, what icy structure does Elsa create on the North Mountain?
questions.explanation: Freed from hiding her powers, Elsa conjures a towering ice palace atop the North Mountain.

questions.question: In Frozen, what is the name of the giant snow guardian Elsa creates?
questions.explanation: Marshmallow is the enormous snow creature Elsa forms to guard her ice palace from intruders.

questions.question: In The Princess and the Frog, what object does Dr. Facilier use to transform Naveen?
questions.explanation: Facilier uses a voodoo talisman filled with Naveen's blood to work his dark magic.

questions.question: In The Little Mermaid, how does Prince Eric defeat the giant Ursula?
questions.explanation: Eric steers a wrecked ship's broken bowsprit into the enormous Ursula, destroying the sea witch.

questions.question: In Frozen, what causes Elsa to reveal her ice powers publicly at her coronation?
questions.explanation: During a heated argument, Anna grabs Elsa's glove, and Elsa's powers erupt in front of the guests.

questions.question: In Tangled, what does Flynn Rider steal at the start of the film?
questions.explanation: Flynn Rider steals the lost princess's crown from the palace, then hides it in Rapunzel's tower.

questions.question: In Tangled, why does Rapunzel agree to leave her tower with Flynn?
questions.explanation: Rapunzel makes a deal with Flynn to guide her to see the floating lanterns on her birthday.

questions.question: In the animated Mulan, what does Mulan use to defeat Shan Yu on the palace rooftop?
questions.explanation: Mulan pins Shan Yu and Mushu launches a firework rocket that blasts the Hun leader away.

questions.question: In Beauty and the Beast, what finally breaks the spell on the Beast?
questions.explanation: The curse breaks when Belle tells the dying Beast she loves him, restoring his human form.

questions.question: In Cinderella, how does the Grand Duke finally identify Cinderella?
questions.explanation: Cinderella is identified when the glass slipper fits her foot perfectly, proving she is the mystery girl.

questions.question: In Aladdin, what is Aladdin's third and final wish to the Genie?
questions.explanation: Instead of a wish for himself, Aladdin uses his last wish to set the Genie free.

questions.question: In Sleeping Beauty, what creature must Prince Phillip slay to reach Aurora?
questions.explanation: Prince Phillip slays Maleficent, who has transformed into a fearsome dragon, to reach Aurora.

questions.question: At the end of Pocahontas, what does Pocahontas choose to do?
questions.explanation: Pocahontas decides to remain with her people as the wounded John Smith sails back to England.

questions.question: In Moana, what happens to Maui's magic fishhook after his battles with Te Kā?
questions.explanation: The fishhook becomes cracked and badly damaged, making Maui fear it will shatter completely.

questions.question: The waltz 'Once Upon a Dream' comes from which Disney film?
answers: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs
questions.explanation: 'Once Upon a Dream,' based on Tchaikovsky's ballet music, is sung by Aurora in Sleeping Beauty.

questions.question: Which character performs 'Be Our Guest' in Beauty and the Beast?
questions.explanation: The candelabra Lumiere leads the dazzling 'Be Our Guest' number to welcome Belle to the castle.

questions.question: Which character sings 'Under the Sea' in The Little Mermaid?
questions.explanation: Sebastian the crab sings 'Under the Sea' to convince Ariel life is better beneath the waves.

questions.question: Which character sings 'Friend Like Me' in Aladdin?
questions.explanation: The Genie performs the show-stopping 'Friend Like Me' to reveal his powers to Aladdin.

questions.question: The opening song 'When Will My Life Begin' belongs to which film?
questions.explanation: Rapunzel sings 'When Will My Life Begin' about her daily routine inside the tower in Tangled.

questions.question: In Frozen, which two characters share the song 'Do You Want to Build a Snowman'?
questions.explanation: Young Anna sings 'Do You Want to Build a Snowman?' to a shut-away Elsa as the sisters grow up.

questions.question: Which princess sings 'Just Around the Riverbend'?
questions.explanation: Pocahontas sings 'Just Around the Riverbend' while canoeing, pondering the choices ahead of her.

questions.question: Which character sings 'Dig a Little Deeper' in The Princess and the Frog?
questions.explanation: The blind voodoo priestess Mama Odie sings 'Dig a Little Deeper' to teach Tiana what she truly needs.

questions.question: Which character sings 'You're Welcome' in Moana?
questions.explanation: The demigod Maui sings 'You're Welcome,' boasting about his many gifts to humankind.

questions.question: In Moana, which character sings the glittering song 'Shiny'?
questions.explanation: Tamatoa, the treasure-covered giant crab of Lalotai, sings 'Shiny' about his love of glittering things.

questions.question: The magic song 'Bibbidi-Bobbidi-Boo' is sung by which character?
questions.explanation: Cinderella's Fairy Godmother sings 'Bibbidi-Bobbidi-Boo' as she transforms the pumpkin and mice.

questions.question: In Disney's Cinderella (1950), which royal is eager for grandchildren and stages the ball?
questions.explanation: The King throws the royal ball hoping his son will finally marry and give him grandchildren.

questions.question: In Sleeping Beauty, which of the three good fairies always dresses in blue?
questions.explanation: Merryweather, the feistiest of the three good fairies, is always dressed in blue.

questions.question: In Frozen, which song do Anna and Hans sing together when they first meet?
answers: For the First Time in Forever
questions.explanation: Anna and Hans duet 'Love Is an Open Door' shortly after meeting at Elsa's coronation.

questions.question: In the animated Beauty and the Beast, what gift from the Beast overjoys book-loving Belle?
questions.explanation: The Beast gives Belle his grand castle library, delighting the book-loving heroine.

questions.question: In Aladdin, what is Aladdin's very first wish to the Genie?
questions.explanation: Aladdin's first wish is to become a prince so he can court Princess Jasmine.

questions.question: In the animated Mulan, what is the name of the captain who trains the army recruits?
questions.explanation: Captain Li Shang trains the new recruits and leads Mulan's unit against the Huns.

questions.question: In The Princess and the Frog, who is the blind voodoo priestess of the bayou?
questions.explanation: Mama Odie is the nearly 200-year-old blind voodoo priestess who helps Tiana and Naveen.

questions.question: In Tangled, what is the name of the tavern full of soft-hearted thugs?
questions.explanation: Rapunzel and Flynn hide in the Snuggly Duckling, a pub full of surprisingly tender thugs.

questions.question: In Snow White, which shy dwarf blushes whenever Snow White speaks to him?
questions.explanation: Bashful is the shy, blushing dwarf who becomes flustered whenever Snow White addresses him.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: Which song does Snow White sing while dreaming that her prince will find her?
answers: A Dream Is a Wish Your Heart Makes
Some Day My Prince Will Come
questions.explanation: Snow White sings 'Some Day My Prince Will Come' while wishing for true love to find her.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: In Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs, what does the Queen order the Huntsman to bring back as proof of her death?
answers: Her heart in a jeweled box
questions.explanation: The Huntsman is ordered to cut out Snow White's heart and bring it back in a box as proof of her death.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: Which of the Seven Dwarfs never speaks a single word throughout the entire film?
questions.explanation: Dopey is the only one of the seven dwarfs who does not speak at any point in the film.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: How does the Evil Queen disguise herself to deliver the poisoned apple to Snow White?
questions.explanation: The Queen transforms herself into an old peddler woman to trick Snow White into eating the poisoned apple.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: Which song does the Fairy Godmother sing while magically preparing Cinderella for the ball?
answers: A Dream Is a Wish Your Heart Makes
questions.explanation: The Fairy Godmother sings 'Bibbidi-Bobbidi-Boo' as she transforms Cinderella's rags into a ball gown.

questions.question: What is the name of the Tremaine household's sheepdog, who often clashes with the cat Lucifer?
questions.explanation: Bruno is the loyal sheepdog who frequently butts heads with Lady Tremaine's cat, Lucifer.

questions.question: According to the Fairy Godmother, at what time will her magic completely reverse itself?
questions.explanation: The Fairy Godmother warns Cinderella that all her magical enchantments will vanish at the stroke of midnight.

questions.question: What common vegetable does the Fairy Godmother transform into Cinderella's coach?
questions.explanation: The Fairy Godmother turns an ordinary pumpkin into Cinderella's magnificent coach for the ball.

questions.question: What is the name of Cinderella's cold and controlling stepmother?
questions.explanation: Lady Tremaine is Cinderella's stepmother, who favors her own daughters and keeps Cinderella as a servant.

questions.question: What name is Princess Aurora given while being secretly raised in a forest cottage?
questions.explanation: To hide her from Maleficent, Aurora is raised under the name Briar Rose until her sixteenth birthday.

questions.question: In Sleeping Beauty, into what creature does Maleficent transform to fight Phillip?
questions.explanation: Maleficent becomes a towering dragon for her final battle with Prince Phillip.

questions.question: What is the name of Maleficent's pet raven, who serves as her spy?
questions.explanation: Diablo is Maleficent's raven, who spies for her and helps locate the sleeping Aurora.

questions.question: What object does Maleficent's curse decree will cause Aurora to fall into a deathlike sleep?
questions.explanation: Maleficent's curse states that Aurora will prick her finger on a spinning wheel's spindle and fall asleep.

questions.question: Which song does Ariel sing in her secret grotto, longing to live among humans?
questions.explanation: Ariel sings 'Part of Your World' in her grotto, expressing her yearning to be part of the human world.

questions.question: Including Ariel, how many daughters does King Triton have in The Little Mermaid?
questions.explanation: King Triton has seven daughters in total, with Ariel being the youngest.

questions.question: What object does Ursula use to trap Ariel's voice after their bargain?
answers: A shell worn as a necklace
questions.explanation: Ursula traps Ariel's singing voice inside a nautilus shell, which she wears as a necklace.

questions.question: What role does Sebastian the crab hold within King Triton's royal court?
questions.explanation: Sebastian serves as King Triton's court composer and is tasked with keeping watch over Ariel.

questions.question: What is the name of King Triton's undersea kingdom?
questions.explanation: Atlantica is the undersea kingdom ruled by King Triton, Ariel's father.

questions.question: What hobby makes Belle stand out as unusual among the townspeople in her village?
questions.explanation: Belle's love of reading books sets her apart and makes the villagers consider her odd.

questions.question: Which song does Lumiere lead the enchanted household staff in performing for Belle?
questions.explanation: Lumiere leads the castle's enchanted servants in the elaborate dinner number 'Be Our Guest.'

questions.question: What household object is Lumiere transformed into under the Enchantress's curse?
questions.explanation: Lumiere, once the castle's maitre d', is transformed into a living candelabra by the Enchantress's curse.

questions.question: Who is Gaston's loyal, comic sidekick who constantly praises him throughout the village?
questions.explanation: LeFou is Gaston's devoted sidekick who follows him and sings his praises throughout the film.

questions.question: What magical object marks the deadline for the Beast to learn to love before his curse becomes permanent?
answers: A candle that never burns out
questions.explanation: The enchanted rose will lose its final petal on the Beast's twenty-first birthday, making the curse permanent.

questions.question: Why does Jasmine disguise herself and sneak out of the palace early in Aladdin?
answers: To escape being forced to choose a suitor
To visit the Cave of Wonders
questions.explanation: Frustrated by the law requiring her to marry a prince, Jasmine disguises herself and slips out of the palace.

questions.question: Which song do Aladdin and Jasmine sing together during their magic carpet ride?
questions.explanation: Aladdin and Jasmine sing 'A Whole New World' as they fly together on the magic carpet.

questions.question: What magical item does Aladdin retrieve from inside the Cave of Wonders that summons the Genie?
questions.explanation: Aladdin discovers a magic oil lamp inside the Cave of Wonders, and rubbing it releases the Genie.

questions.question: In the animated Aladdin, what is the name of Jafar's scheming parrot?
questions.explanation: Iago is Jafar's sarcastic, talkative pet parrot.

questions.question: What kind of animal is Meeko, one of Pocahontas's mischievous animal companions?
questions.explanation: Meeko is a mischievous raccoon who accompanies Pocahontas throughout the film.

questions.question: What is the name of Pocahontas's father, the chief who leads their people?
questions.explanation: Chief Powhatan is Pocahontas's father and the respected leader of his people.

questions.question: What choice does Pocahontas make regarding the wounded John Smith at the end of the film?
answers: She leaves with him for England
She chooses to stay with her people
questions.explanation: Pocahontas chooses to remain with her people rather than sail to England with the wounded John Smith.

questions.question: Who is the greedy governor leading the English settlers' search for gold in Pocahontas?
questions.explanation: Governor Ratcliffe leads the Jamestown expedition and is driven by his obsession with finding gold.

questions.question: What does Mulan do to prevent her aging father from being drafted into the Imperial Army?
answers: She pays officials to excuse him
She hides her father from the army
She disguises herself as a man and takes his place
questions.explanation: Mulan secretly takes her father's place in the army by disguising herself as a soldier named Ping.

questions.question: What is the name of the small, talkative dragon sent to guide and protect Mulan?
questions.explanation: Mushu is the tiny dragon sent by Mulan's ancestors to watch over and guide her.

questions.question: What is the name of the ruthless Hun leader who invades China in Mulan?
questions.explanation: Shan Yu is the ruthless leader of the Hun army invading China throughout the film.

questions.question: Who in Mulan's family gives her a cricket for good luck before she visits the Matchmaker?
questions.explanation: Grandmother Fa gives Mulan a cricket named Cri-Kee, believing it will bring her good luck.

questions.question: What important meeting does Mulan disastrously attend early in the film, meant to secure her a husband?
questions.explanation: Mulan's visit to the Matchmaker ends in disaster when her attempts to impress go badly wrong.

questions.question: What is Tiana's lifelong dream that drives much of the plot in The Princess and the Frog?
questions.explanation: Tiana dreams of opening her own restaurant, a goal inspired by cooking with her late father.

questions.question: What kind of creature is Louis, one of Tiana's companions on her journey through the bayou?
questions.explanation: Louis is a trumpet-playing alligator who dreams of performing with a jazz band.

questions.question: What is the name of Tiana's wealthy best friend since childhood in New Orleans?
questions.explanation: Charlotte La Bouff, daughter of 'Big Daddy' La Bouff, is Tiana's lifelong best friend.

questions.question: What is the name of the voodoo sorcerer who tricks Prince Naveen in The Princess and the Frog?
questions.explanation: Dr. Facilier, known as the 'Shadow Man,' is the voodoo sorcerer who deceives Prince Naveen.

questions.question: What is the fictional homeland of Prince Naveen before he arrives in New Orleans?
questions.explanation: Prince Naveen hails from the fictional kingdom of Maldonia before traveling to New Orleans.

questions.question: How old does Rapunzel turn on the night of the floating lanterns in Tangled?
questions.explanation: Rapunzel's tower escape culminates on the night of her eighteenth birthday, when the lanterns are released.

questions.question: What kind of animal is Pascal, Rapunzel's loyal companion throughout Tangled?
questions.explanation: Pascal is Rapunzel's color-changing chameleon companion who accompanies her everywhere.

questions.question: Why does Mother Gothel keep Rapunzel hidden away in a secluded tower?
answers: To protect her from a kingdom-wide curse
To use her magic hair to stay young
To keep her safe from Flynn Rider
To train her as a royal healer
questions.explanation: Mother Gothel exploits the youth-restoring magic in Rapunzel's hair to keep herself perpetually young.

questions.question: What is the name of the kingdom Rapunzel is revealed to be the lost princess of?
questions.explanation: Rapunzel is revealed to be the long-lost princess of the kingdom of Corona.

questions.question: What real name does Flynn Rider reveal to Rapunzel in a moment of trust?
questions.explanation: Flynn Rider confides to Rapunzel that his real name is actually Eugene Fitzherbert.

questions.question: What skill does Merida use to win her own hand in the archery contest arranged by the clans?
questions.explanation: Merida enters the archery contest herself, outshooting every suitor to claim her own hand.

questions.question: What are the names of Merida's three young triplet brothers in Brave?
