questions.sourceTitle: Darby O'Gill and the Little People

questions.question: In The Shaggy Dog, into which breed of dog does teenager Wilby Daniels magically transform?
questions.explanation: An ancient ring's spell turns Wilby into an Old English Sheepdog in Disney's 1959 comedy.

questions.question: Which 1950 film was Walt Disney's first fully live-action feature?
questions.explanation: Disney's 1950 Treasure Island, starring Robert Newton as Long John Silver, was the studio's first all-live-action feature.

questions.question: In Disney's 1961 Babes in Toyland, what is the name of the villain scheming to marry Mary Contrary?
questions.explanation: The greedy villain Barnaby, played by Ray Bolger, plots to wed Mary Contrary for her inheritance.

questions.question: In Greyfriars Bobby, what kind of dog keeps vigil at his master's grave in Edinburgh?
questions.explanation: The loyal Skye Terrier Bobby famously guards his owner's grave for years in the 1961 Disney film.

questions.question: In Pollyanna, the cheerful heroine becomes paralyzed after falling from what?
questions.explanation: Pollyanna loses the use of her legs after falling from a tree while climbing to her attic room.

questions.question: In The Absent-Minded Professor, at which college does Professor Brainard teach?
questions.explanation: Professor Ned Brainard teaches at Medfield College, a fictional school revisited in later Disney comedies.

questions.question: In the 1961 Parent Trap, at which summer camp do the separated twins first meet?
questions.explanation: The identical twins played by Hayley Mills meet at Camp Inch in the 1961 original.

questions.question: In Enchanted, who plays the wicked Queen Narissa?
questions.explanation: Susan Sarandon plays Queen Narissa, the villain who banishes Giselle to New York City.

questions.question: In Holes, what nickname do the other boys give Stanley Yelnats at Camp Green Lake?
questions.explanation: The boys at Camp Green Lake nickname Stanley 'Caveman' after he stands up to the group.

questions.question: In Jungle Cruise, what healing legend do Frank and Lily search for in the Amazon?
questions.explanation: They seek the Tears of the Moon, petals from a hidden tree said to cure any illness.

questions.question: In The Sorcerer's Apprentice, what is the name of Nicolas Cage's master sorcerer?
questions.explanation: Nicolas Cage plays Balthazar Blake, a centuries-old sorcerer seeking Merlin's heir in modern New York.

questions.question: In Prince of Persia: The Sands of Time, what power does the Dagger of Time grant its holder?
questions.explanation: The Dagger releases the Sands of Time, letting its holder rewind events by a short span.
questions.sourceTitle: Prince of Persia: The Sands of Time

questions.question: In John Carter, what do the inhabitants of Mars call their own planet?
questions.explanation: Mars is known as Barsoom to its natives in the film based on Edgar Rice Burroughs' novels.

questions.question: In Million Dollar Arm, to which country does the sports agent travel to recruit pitchers?
questions.explanation: Agent JB Bernstein holds a talent contest in India to find cricket bowlers who can pitch baseball.

questions.question: In McFarland, USA, which sport does Kevin Costner's coach lead the students in?
questions.explanation: Coach Jim White builds a champion cross-country running team from farm-working students in McFarland, California.

questions.question: In the 2019 live-action The Lion King, who voices the adult Simba?
questions.explanation: Donald Glover voices adult Simba in the 2019 photorealistic remake directed by Jon Favreau.

questions.question: Who directed the 2019 live-action Aladdin?
questions.explanation: Guy Ritchie directed the 2019 Aladdin, with Will Smith as the Genie and Mena Massoud as Aladdin.

questions.question: Which animated sidekick is deliberately absent from the 2020 live-action Mulan?
questions.explanation: The 2020 remake dropped the comedic dragon Mushu, replacing him with a symbolic phoenix.

questions.question: In the 2016 live-action The Jungle Book, who voices the tiger Shere Khan?
questions.explanation: Idris Elba voices the menacing tiger Shere Khan in Jon Favreau's 2016 photorealistic remake.

questions.question: Who plays the Mad Hatter in Tim Burton's 2010 Alice in Wonderland?
questions.explanation: Johnny Depp plays the Mad Hatter in Tim Burton's 2010 reimagining of Alice in Wonderland.

questions.question: Who plays Cruella de Vil in the 1996 live-action 101 Dalmatians?
questions.explanation: Glenn Close plays the fur-obsessed villain Cruella de Vil in the 1996 live-action 101 Dalmatians.

questions.question: In the 2019 live-action Dumbo, who plays entertainment tycoon V.A. Vandevere?
questions.explanation: Michael Keaton plays the ambitious showman V.A. Vandevere in Tim Burton's 2019 Dumbo.

questions.question: In The Curse of the Black Pearl, in which port town does Elizabeth Swann live?
questions.explanation: Elizabeth lives in Port Royal, Jamaica, where her father serves as governor.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl

questions.question: In At World's End, at which pirate stronghold does the Brethren Court gather?
questions.explanation: The nine pirate lords convene at Shipwreck Cove to decide how to face Cutler Beckett.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: At World's End

questions.question: In At World's End, who is the ruthless East India Trading Company chief hunting the pirates?
questions.explanation: Lord Cutler Beckett commands the East India Trading Company's campaign to wipe out piracy.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: At World's End

questions.question: In Dead Man's Chest, what enormous sea monster does Davy Jones command?
questions.explanation: Davy Jones unleashes the tentacled Kraken to drag ships and debtors to the ocean depths.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest

questions.question: In Dead Man's Chest, what mark on Jack's hand signals the Kraken is hunting him?
questions.explanation: The Black Spot appears on Jack's palm, marking his blood debt owed to Davy Jones.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest

questions.question: In On Stranger Tides, what is the name of Blackbeard's ship?
questions.explanation: Blackbeard commands the Queen Anne's Revenge, whose magical rigging can subdue its crew.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: On Stranger Tides

questions.question: In On Stranger Tides, the Fountain of Youth ritual requires two chalices and what from a mermaid?
questions.explanation: The ritual demands a mermaid's tear placed in one silver chalice to transfer years of life.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: On Stranger Tides

questions.question: In Dead Men Tell No Tales, astronomer Carina Smyth is revealed to be whose daughter?
questions.explanation: Carina Smyth is revealed to be the long-lost daughter of Captain Hector Barbossa.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Men Tell No Tales

questions.question: In Mary Poppins, at what address does the Banks family live?
questions.explanation: The Banks family resides at 17 Cherry Tree Lane in Edwardian London.

questions.question: In Mary Poppins, elderly bank chairman Mr. Dawes Sr. is later said to have died doing what?
questions.explanation: Mr. Dawes Sr. dies laughing after finally understanding Mr. Banks's joke about a wooden leg named Smith.

questions.question: In Mary Poppins, into what do Mary, Bert and the children jump to reach the countryside?
questions.explanation: They leap into Bert's chalk pavement drawing during the animated 'Jolly Holiday' sequence.

questions.question: In Mary Poppins, the bird woman sells crumbs on the steps of which London landmark?
questions.explanation: The 'Feed the Birds' woman sells tuppence bags of crumbs on the steps of St. Paul's Cathedral.

questions.question: In Mary Poppins Returns, what is the name of the lamplighter who helps Mary and the children?
questions.explanation: Jack, a cheerful lamplighter played by Lin-Manuel Miranda, assists Mary throughout the sequel.

questions.question: In Mary Poppins Returns, who plays Cousin Topsy, whose home turns upside down?
questions.explanation: Meryl Streep plays Cousin Topsy, whose fix-it shop flips upside down every second Wednesday.

questions.question: In Mary Poppins Returns, which screen legend plays the balloon lady near the end?
questions.explanation: Angela Lansbury plays the balloon lady whose balloons let Londoners float during the finale.

questions.question: On The Muppet Show, who is Dr. Bunsen Honeydew's long-suffering lab assistant?
questions.explanation: Beaker is Dr. Bunsen Honeydew's timid assistant who suffers his disastrous Muppet Labs experiments.

questions.question: On The Muppet Show, what catchphrase does comedian Fozzie Bear use during his stand-up act?
questions.explanation: Fozzie Bear punctuates his corny jokes with his signature 'Wocka wocka!' catchphrase.

questions.question: In The Muppet Movie, Doc Hopper wants Kermit to be the spokesfrog for what product?
questions.explanation: Doc Hopper relentlessly pursues Kermit to advertise his chain of fried frog legs restaurants.

questions.question: In The Great Muppet Caper, Kermit and Fozzie play what relation to each other?
questions.explanation: As a running gag, reporters Kermit and Fozzie are presented as identical twin brothers.

questions.question: In The Muppet Christmas Carol, which Dickens character does Kermit the Frog play?
questions.explanation: Kermit plays Bob Cratchit, Scrooge's underpaid clerk and Tiny Tim's devoted father.

questions.question: In Muppet Treasure Island, which character does Kermit the Frog play?
questions.explanation: Kermit plays the upright Captain Abraham Smollett, commander of the treasure-seeking Hispaniola.

questions.question: In The Muppets (2011), the Muppet superfan Walter has a human brother named what?
questions.explanation: Walter's human brother Gary, played by Jason Segel, helps reunite the Muppets to save their studio.

questions.question: In the original Tron, who is the commanding program that enforces the Master Control Program's rule?
questions.explanation: Sark, played by David Warner, serves as the Master Control Program's brutal military commander.

questions.question: In Tron, what is the name of Kevin Flynn's video arcade?
questions.explanation: Kevin Flynn runs Flynn's Arcade, where he is later digitized into the computer world.

questions.question: In Tron, which game did Kevin Flynn create that Ed Dillinger stole to rise at ENCOM?
questions.explanation: Dillinger stole Flynn's game Space Paranoids to claim credit and become an ENCOM executive.

questions.question: In Tron, what term describes a program being destroyed in the digital world?
questions.explanation: A program that is destroyed is said to be 'derezzed,' breaking apart into fading blocks.

questions.question: In Tron: Legacy, which flamboyant nightclub program, played by Michael Sheen, betrays Sam?
questions.explanation: Castor, who also goes by Zuse, runs the End of Line Club before betraying Sam to CLU's forces.

questions.question: In Tron: Legacy, the masked enforcer Rinzler is revealed to be which reprogrammed character?
questions.explanation: Rinzler is revealed to be the hero program Tron, corrupted and reprogrammed to serve CLU.

questions.question: In National Treasure, how is the hidden map on the back of the Declaration revealed?
questions.explanation: Ben applies lemon juice and gentle heat to reveal the invisible-ink map on the Declaration's back.

questions.question: In National Treasure, what is the name of the ship found frozen in the Arctic holding an early clue?
questions.explanation: Ben and his team find the frozen ship Charlotte, where a meerschaum pipe holds the next clue.

questions.question: In National Treasure, whose invented spectacles are needed to read the Declaration's hidden clues?
questions.explanation: Benjamin Franklin's multi-lensed spectacles reveal colored clues written on the Declaration.

questions.question: In National Treasure, which medieval order is said to have first amassed the fabled treasure?
questions.explanation: The film says the Knights Templar amassed the treasure, later safeguarded by the Freemasons.

questions.question: In National Treasure, what is the name of the FBI agent who pursues, then aids, Ben Gates?
questions.explanation: FBI Agent Sadusky, a fellow Freemason played by Harvey Keitel, ultimately helps Ben Gates.

questions.question: In Book of Secrets, which treasure hunter played by Ed Harris is Ben Gates's rival?
questions.explanation: Mitch Wilkinson, played by Ed Harris, races Ben to find Cibola while defending his ancestor's name.

questions.question: In Book of Secrets, a clue hides in twin desks at the White House and Buckingham Palace. Which desk?
questions.explanation: The clue is hidden in the twin Resolute desks, one in the Oval Office and one at Buckingham Palace.

questions.question: In High School Musical, Troy Bolton is captain of the Wildcats in which sport?
questions.explanation: Troy Bolton captains the East High Wildcats basketball team before discovering his love of singing.

questions.question: In Camp Rock, what is the name of the pop star Mitchie befriends at camp?
questions.explanation: Mitchie bonds with pop star Shane Gray, played by Joe Jonas, over a mystery singing voice.

questions.question: In the Disney Channel movie Smart House, what is the name of the home's talking computer?
questions.explanation: The automated house is run by PAT, an AI that grows overprotective of the family.

questions.question: In Johnny Tsunami, the Hawaiian surfer moves to Vermont and takes up which sport?
questions.explanation: After moving to snowy Vermont, surfer Johnny Kapahaala takes up snowboarding.

questions.question: In Brink!, Andy 'Brink' Brinker and his friends compete in which sport?
questions.explanation: Brink and his crew are aggressive inline skaters who ride for the love of the sport.

questions.question: In Halloweentown, what is the name of Marnie's witch grandmother, played by Debbie Reynolds?
questions.explanation: Debbie Reynolds plays Aggie Cromwell, the witch grandmother who introduces Marnie to Halloweentown.

questions.question: In Maleficent (2014), what is the name of the raven Maleficent transforms into a servant?
questions.explanation: Maleficent rescues and transforms the raven Diaval, who becomes her loyal shape-shifting companion.

questions.question: In Cruella, what are the names of the two thieves who become Cruella's accomplices?
questions.explanation: Jasper and Horace, petty crooks who raised Estella, become Cruella's partners in her schemes.

questions.question: In Enchanted, what is the name of Giselle's loyal chipmunk friend?
questions.explanation: Pip the chipmunk follows Giselle from Andalasia and helps foil Queen Narissa's plans.

questions.question: In Jungle Cruise, what is the name of skipper Frank Wolff's pet jaguar?
questions.explanation: Frank's trained jaguar Proxima helps stage frights for tourists on his jungle cruise.

questions.question: In The Sorcerer's Apprentice, what is the name of Balthazar's physics-student apprentice?
questions.explanation: Dave Stutler, a college physics student, is trained by Balthazar as Merlin's prophesied heir.

questions.question: In Descendants, the son of Belle and the Beast who becomes king of Auradon is named what?
questions.explanation: Prince Ben, son of Belle and the Beast, invites the villains' children to Auradon Prep.

questions.question: In Old Yeller, Travis must put down the beloved dog after it contracts which disease?
questions.explanation: Old Yeller contracts rabies protecting the family from a rabid wolf, forcing Travis to shoot him.

questions.question: At Camp Green Lake in Holes, each boy must dig one hole of what size every day?
questions.explanation: Each boy digs a hole five feet deep and five feet wide daily, supposedly to build character.

questions.question: In Enchanted's climax, the villain Narissa becomes a dragon atop what kind of structure?
questions.explanation: Narissa transforms into a dragon and climbs a Manhattan skyscraper during the stormy finale.

questions.question: In The Parent Trap (1998), how do Hallie and Annie realize they are twin sisters?
answers: Halves of the same torn photo
questions.explanation: The girls discover each holds half of the same torn photograph of their parents.

questions.question: In Escape to Witch Mountain, the orphans Tony and Tia possess what kind of abilities?
questions.explanation: Tony and Tia have psychic powers, including telekinesis, tied to their mysterious alien origins.

questions.question: In Bedknobs and Broomsticks, the magic bed carries the group to which island of animated animals?
questions.explanation: The enchanted bed travels to the animated Isle of Naboombu, ruled by a lion king.

questions.question: Which actor plays the vengeful ghost Captain Salazar in Dead Men Tell No Tales?
questions.explanation: Javier Bardem plays the undead Spanish captain Armando Salazar, who hunts Jack Sparrow.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Men Tell No Tales

questions.question: In Muppets Most Wanted, what is the alias of the criminal played by Ricky Gervais?
questions.explanation: Ricky Gervais plays Dominic Badguy, a tour manager who is secretly Constantine's accomplice.

questions.question: In Book of Secrets, which fabled page of the President's secret book does Ben seek?
questions.explanation: Ben chases the legendary page 47, said to hold a world-changing secret in the President's Book.

questions.question: In Old Yeller, what is the name of Travis's mischievous younger brother?
questions.explanation: Arliss Coates is Travis's little brother, whose antics the stray dog Old Yeller often watches over.

questions.question: Which iconic castle sits at the center of Magic Kingdom at Walt Disney World?
questions.explanation: Cinderella Castle is the centerpiece of Magic Kingdom, standing at the heart of the park.

questions.question: Which castle serves as the icon of Disneyland Park in California?
questions.explanation: Sleeping Beauty Castle has stood at the heart of Disneyland Park since it opened in 1955.

questions.question: Which Magic Kingdom attraction sends riders on an indoor roller coaster through outer space?
questions.explanation: Space Mountain is a high-speed indoor coaster located in Tomorrowland at Magic Kingdom.

questions.question: Which Magic Kingdom land is home to Space Mountain and the PeopleMover?
questions.explanation: Tomorrowland at Magic Kingdom features futuristic attractions like Space Mountain.

questions.question: Which boat ride features hundreds of singing dolls representing cultures around the world?
questions.explanation: it's a small world is a boat ride featuring hundreds of singing dolls celebrating world cultures.

questions.question: Which attraction features skippers narrating a comedic voyage through rivers of the world?
questions.explanation: Jungle Cruise features skippers guiding boats past animatronic animals with scripted comedic narration.

questions.question: Which Magic Kingdom land is themed to a spooky Southern mansion filled with 999 ghosts?
questions.explanation: The Haunted Mansion is located in Liberty Square, themed after colonial America.

questions.question: Which pirate-themed boat ride inspired a popular movie franchise starring Johnny Depp?
questions.explanation: Pirates of the Caribbean, a classic dark boat ride, inspired the blockbuster film series.

questions.question: EPCOT's giant geodesic sphere icon is known by what name?
questions.explanation: Spaceship Earth is the massive geodesic sphere that serves as EPCOT's iconic entrance attraction.

questions.question: Which section of EPCOT features pavilions themed to countries from around the world?
questions.explanation: World Showcase surrounds a lagoon at EPCOT with pavilions representing eleven different countries.

questions.question: Which country pavilion at EPCOT's World Showcase features a replica of the Eiffel Tower?
questions.explanation: The France pavilion at EPCOT's World Showcase includes a scaled-down model of the Eiffel Tower.

questions.question: Which EPCOT pavilion is home to the attraction Soarin' Around the World?
questions.explanation: Soarin' Around the World is a hang-gliding simulator ride located in The Land pavilion at EPCOT.

questions.question: Which Hollywood Studios attraction drops riders in a runaway elevator inside a haunted hotel?
answers: The Twilight Zone Tower of Terror
questions.explanation: The Twilight Zone Tower of Terror simulates a haunted elevator plunging at the fictional Hollywood Tower Hotel.
questions.sourceTitle: The Twilight Zone Tower of Terror

questions.question: Which Hollywood Studios coaster launches riders through loops set to rock music?
questions.explanation: Rock 'n' Roller Coaster Starring Aerosmith launches riders through loops set to rock music.

questions.question: Which land at Disney's Hollywood Studios is themed after a galaxy far, far away?
questions.explanation: Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge is themed to the planet Batuu, set in the Star Wars universe.

questions.question: Which giant artificial tree serves as the icon of Disney's Animal Kingdom?
questions.explanation: The Tree of Life stands at the center of Animal Kingdom and is carved with hundreds of animal figures.

questions.question: Which Animal Kingdom attraction takes guests on a truck ride through African savannas?
questions.explanation: Kilimanjaro Safaris takes guests on an open-truck ride through a recreated African savanna.

questions.question: Which Animal Kingdom coaster is themed around a legendary snow creature in the Himalayas?
questions.explanation: Expedition Everest features a mountain coaster themed around the legend of the Yeti.

questions.question: Which Animal Kingdom land is themed after the movie Avatar?
questions.explanation: Pandora - The World of Avatar features bioluminescent plants and floating mountains inspired by the film.

questions.question: Which Disney California Adventure land is themed around the Pixar film Cars?
questions.explanation: Cars Land recreates Radiator Springs and features the attraction Radiator Springs Racers.

questions.question: Which Disneyland Park mountain attraction sends bobsleds racing around a snowy peak?
questions.explanation: The Matterhorn Bobsleds attraction sends sleds racing through a scale model of the Swiss mountain.

questions.question: Which Disneyland Park attraction was the world's first tubular steel roller coaster?
questions.explanation: The Matterhorn Bobsleds, opened in 1959, was the world's first tubular steel roller coaster track.

questions.question: Which park at the Tokyo Disney Resort features Cinderella Castle at its center?
questions.explanation: Tokyo Disneyland, which opened in 1983, features Cinderella Castle at the center of the park.

questions.question: Which Disney park is themed entirely around nautical exploration and features a giant volcano?
questions.explanation: Tokyo DisneySea is built around a large volcano called Mount Prometheus and various nautical ports.

questions.question: In which country is Disneyland Paris located?
questions.explanation: Disneyland Paris is located in Marne-la-Vallee, a suburb east of Paris, France.

questions.question: Which Disney park opened in 2005 in the Chinese special administrative region of Hong Kong?
questions.explanation: Hong Kong Disneyland opened in 2005, becoming the second Disney theme park resort in Asia.

questions.question: Which Disney park, opened in 2016 near Shanghai, is the first Disney park in mainland China?
questions.explanation: Shanghai Disneyland opened in 2016, becoming the first and only Disney theme park in mainland China.

questions.question: Which mode of transportation glides above Walt Disney World connecting several resorts and parks?
questions.explanation: The Disney Skyliner is a gondola lift system connecting Hollywood Studios, EPCOT, and nearby resorts.

questions.question: Which elevated train system connects Magic Kingdom to the Transportation and Ticket Center?
questions.explanation: The Walt Disney World Monorail system links Magic Kingdom with the Transportation and Ticket Center and resorts.

questions.question: Which Tomorrowland ride glides guests slowly above the land on an elevated track?
questions.explanation: The WEDWay PeopleMover carries guests on a slow, elevated loop around Tomorrowland at Magic Kingdom.

questions.question: Which Disney World resort is themed after the South Pacific with tiki torches and tropical gardens?
questions.explanation: Disney's Polynesian Village Resort features South Pacific theming, longhouses, and tropical landscaping.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World resort features a monorail that travels directly through its main building?
questions.explanation: The Walt Disney World Monorail passes directly through the atrium of Disney's Contemporary Resort.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World resort is themed after grand national park lodges of the American West?
questions.explanation: Disney's Wilderness Lodge draws its theme from rustic National Park lodges, featuring log-cabin architecture.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World resort places guests near live savanna animals visible from many rooms?
questions.explanation: Disney's Animal Kingdom Lodge overlooks savannas where giraffes and zebras roam near guest room balconies.

questions.question: Which classic parade lit up Main Street U.S.A. at Disneyland with illuminated floats starting in 1972?
questions.explanation: The Main Street Electrical Parade debuted in 1972, dazzling guests with thousands of sparkling lights.

questions.question: Which nighttime show features Mickey battling Disney villains using fire and water effects?
questions.explanation: Fantasmic! is a nighttime show featuring Mickey Mouse using his imagination to battle Disney villains.

questions.question: What is the name of Walt Disney's brother who co-founded The Walt Disney Company?
questions.explanation: Roy O. Disney co-founded the company with his brother Walt and later completed Walt Disney World.

questions.question: In what year did Disneyland Park in California first open to the public?
questions.explanation: Disneyland Park opened its gates in Anaheim, California, on July 17, 1955.

questions.question: In what year did the Magic Kingdom at Walt Disney World open?
questions.explanation: Magic Kingdom opened on October 1, 1971, as the first park at Walt Disney World.

questions.question: In what year did EPCOT open at Walt Disney World?
questions.explanation: EPCOT opened on October 1, 1982, as the second theme park at Walt Disney World.

questions.question: Which retired Magic Kingdom attraction was based on a Jules Verne submarine voyage?
questions.explanation: 20,000 Leagues Under the Sea took guests on a simulated submarine voyage inspired by the Jules Verne novel.

questions.question: Which retired EPCOT attraction showed a whimsical vision of future daily life through a home?
questions.explanation: Horizons was a beloved EPCOT attraction that showed possible visions of daily life in the future.

questions.question: Which retired Magic Kingdom ride was based on a character who loved wild motorcar rides?
questions.explanation: Mr. Toad's Wild Ride, based on The Wind in the Willows, closed at Magic Kingdom in 1998.

questions.question: Which Fantasyland attraction lets guests soar over London with Peter Pan and friends?
questions.explanation: Peter Pan's Flight is a suspended dark ride that soars guests above a nighttime London skyline.

questions.question: Which Frontierland attraction is a runaway mine train coaster themed to the Old West?
questions.explanation: Big Thunder Mountain Railroad is a family coaster themed as a runaway mine train through the Old West.

questions.question: Which Magic Kingdom land features Big Thunder Mountain Railroad and Country Bear Jamboree?
questions.explanation: Frontierland is themed to the American frontier and is home to Big Thunder Mountain Railroad.

questions.question: Which Magic Kingdom land includes the Swiss Family Treehouse and Jungle Cruise?
questions.explanation: Adventureland is themed to exotic jungles and features attractions like the Jungle Cruise.

questions.question: Which street welcomes guests at the entrance of Magic Kingdom, styled after a small town?
questions.explanation: Main Street U.S.A. greets guests at the entrance of Magic Kingdom, styled after a turn-of-the-century town.

questions.question: Which Hollywood Studios street is home to Rock 'n' Roller Coaster and Tower of Terror?
questions.explanation: Sunset Boulevard at Hollywood Studios is home to Rock 'n' Roller Coaster and Tower of Terror.

questions.question: What is the name of the entrance street at Disney's Hollywood Studios lined with shops?
questions.explanation: Hollywood Boulevard welcomes guests into Disney's Hollywood Studios, styled after 1930s Hollywood.

questions.question: Which EPCOT attraction takes guests on a boat tour through real working greenhouses?
questions.explanation: Living with the Land takes guests on a boat tour through real greenhouses inside The Land pavilion.

questions.question: Which attraction lets guests design and test-drive a concept vehicle at high speed at EPCOT?
questions.explanation: Test Track lets riders design and speed around a track testing a concept vehicle at EPCOT.

questions.question: Which classic Fantasyland ride spins guests around on ornately painted horses?
questions.explanation: Prince Charming Regal Carrousel is a beautifully restored antique carousel in Magic Kingdom's Fantasyland.

questions.question: Which teacup-themed spinning ride is a Fantasyland favorite at multiple Disney parks?
questions.explanation: The Mad Tea Party spins guests in giant teacups, themed to Alice in Wonderland's unbirthday party.

questions.question: Which flying elephant attraction is a beloved classic for young guests in Fantasyland?
questions.explanation: Dumbo the Flying Elephant lets guests soar in elephant-shaped vehicles around a central hub.

questions.question: Which Magic Kingdom attraction is a steam-powered riverboat that circles Tom Sawyer Island?
questions.explanation: The Liberty Square Riverboat is a steam-powered paddlewheel boat that circles the Rivers of America.

questions.question: Which Animal Kingdom attraction is a 3D theater show set beneath the Tree of Life?
answers: It's Tough to be a Bug!
Finding Nemo: The Big Blue... and Beyond!
questions.explanation: It's Tough to be a Bug! is a 3D theater show located beneath the Tree of Life at Animal Kingdom.
questions.sourceTitle: It's Tough to be a Bug!

questions.question: Which attraction originally debuted at Disney California Adventure, simulating flight over scenery?
questions.explanation: Soarin' originally debuted at Disney California Adventure, simulating flight over famous California scenery.

questions.question: Which Disney California Adventure land is themed to a boardwalk with an observation wheel?
questions.explanation: Pixar Pier is themed like a seaside boardwalk and features the Pixar Pal-A-Round observation wheel.

questions.question: Which classic dark ride retold the story of Snow White before closing at Magic Kingdom?
answers: The Many Adventures of Winnie the Pooh
questions.explanation: Snow White's Scary Adventures was a dark ride retelling the classic fairy tale at Magic Kingdom.

questions.question: Which Fantasyland coaster takes riders through a mine alongside animatronic dwarfs?
questions.explanation: Seven Dwarfs Mine Train is a family coaster that swings through scenes with the Seven Dwarfs.

questions.question: Which Magic Kingdom attraction takes guests through the Hundred Acre Wood with a honey-loving bear?
answers: Under the Sea: Journey of the Little Mermaid
The Many Adventures of Winnie the Pooh
questions.explanation: The Many Adventures of Winnie the Pooh is a dark ride through scenes from the Hundred Acre Wood.
questions.sourceTitle: The Many Adventures of Winnie the Pooh

questions.question: Which Magic Kingdom dark ride takes guests under the sea with Ariel and her friends?
answers: Under the Sea: Journey of the Little Mermaid
questions.explanation: Under the Sea: Journey of the Little Mermaid is a Fantasyland dark ride based on the animated film.
questions.sourceTitle: Under the Sea: Journey of the Little Mermaid

questions.question: Which fireworks show has been performed above Cinderella Castle at Magic Kingdom?
questions.explanation: Happily Ever After is a fireworks show set to music that has been performed above Cinderella Castle.

questions.question: Which nighttime water and light show combines fountains, lights, and film at Disney California Adventure?
questions.explanation: World of Color is a nighttime show combining fountains, lights, and film projections on Paradise Bay.

questions.question: Which resort hotel at Walt Disney World is known for its Victorian architecture and white facade?
questions.explanation: Disney's Grand Floridian Resort & Spa features elegant Victorian-style architecture reminiscent of a bygone era.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World resort is themed with New England lighthouses and nautical decor?
questions.explanation: Disney's Yacht Club Resort features New England nautical theming with a lighthouse and boardwalk setting.

questions.question: Which form of transportation carries guests across the Seven Seas Lagoon to Magic Kingdom?
questions.explanation: Ferry boats carry guests across the Seven Seas Lagoon between the Transportation and Ticket Center and Magic Kingdom.

questions.question: Which aerial cable car attraction once connected Fantasyland and Tomorrowland at Disneyland?
questions.explanation: The Skyway was an aerial tramway connecting Fantasyland and Tomorrowland at Disneyland until 1994.

questions.question: Which spinning Tomorrowland attraction lifts guests in rockets high above the land?
questions.explanation: Astro Orbiter is a spinning ride with rocket vehicles that lift high above Tomorrowland.

questions.question: Which rotating theater attraction shows a family living through decades of technology change?
questions.explanation: Walt Disney's Carousel of Progress is a rotating theater show tracing a family through decades of technology.

questions.question: Which Liberty Square attraction uses animatronic figures to present every U.S. president?
questions.explanation: The Hall of Presidents is a theater show featuring animatronic figures of every U.S. president.

questions.question: Which Frontierland show features a troupe of singing animatronic bears?
questions.explanation: Country Bear Jamboree is a classic Frontierland show starring a cast of singing animatronic bears.

questions.question: Which Adventureland attraction is a musical show hosted by animatronic tropical birds?
questions.explanation: The Enchanted Tiki Room is a musical show starring animatronic tropical birds, flowers, and tikis.

questions.question: Which Adventureland walk-through attraction is inspired by a family shipwrecked on a jungle island?
questions.explanation: The Swiss Family Treehouse is a walk-through attraction inspired by the novel Swiss Family Robinson.

questions.question: Which retired log flume ride featured a steep plunge and was themed to Br'er Rabbit folk tales?
questions.explanation: Splash Mountain was a log flume ride with a steep drop, based on Br'er Rabbit folk tales, later retired.

questions.question: Which Animal Kingdom raft ride sends guests down a rushing river through a rainforest?
questions.explanation: Kali River Rapids is a whitewater raft ride themed to a rainforest at Disney's Animal Kingdom.

questions.question: Which Disney California Adventure raft ride sends guests through rushing whitewater rapids?

questions.question: Which show at Animal Kingdom is presented underneath the Tree of Life?
answers: It's Tough to be a Bug!
questions.explanation: It's Tough to be a Bug! is a 3D theater show located inside the Tree of Life at Animal Kingdom.
questions.sourceTitle: It's Tough to be a Bug!

questions.question: DinoLand U.S.A. at Animal Kingdom is themed around which field of science?
questions.explanation: DinoLand U.S.A. is themed around paleontology and the study of dinosaur fossils.

questions.question: Na'vi River Journey is a boat ride found in which Animal Kingdom land?
questions.explanation: Na'vi River Journey is a gentle boat ride through a bioluminescent forest in Pandora.

questions.question: Which Animal Kingdom land recreates a rural village inspired by several Asian cultures?
questions.explanation: The Asia land at Animal Kingdom is themed to a fictional village drawing on multiple Asian cultures.

questions.question: What castle serves as the icon of Disneyland Park in Anaheim?
questions.explanation: Sleeping Beauty Castle is the centerpiece icon of Disneyland Park in Anaheim, California.

questions.question: Which park first introduced Pirates of the Caribbean to the world in 1967?
questions.explanation: Pirates of the Caribbean debuted at Disneyland Park in Anaheim in 1967.

questions.question: Indiana Jones Adventure is located in which Disneyland Park land?
questions.explanation: Indiana Jones Adventure is a jeep-based dark ride located in Disneyland's Adventureland.

questions.question: The Matterhorn Bobsleds attraction is themed after a mountain located in which country?
questions.explanation: The Matterhorn Bobsleds is themed after the Matterhorn, a mountain on the Swiss-Italian border.

questions.question: What is the name of the steam train that circles Disneyland Park?
questions.explanation: The Disneyland Railroad is a steam train that loops around the perimeter of Disneyland Park.

questions.question: Which land at Disneyland Park is themed to French Quarter architecture?
questions.explanation: New Orleans Square recreates 19th-century French Quarter architecture at Disneyland Park.

questions.question: Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge at Disneyland Park is set on which fictional planet?
questions.explanation: Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge is set on the planet Batuu, home to the Black Spire Outpost.

questions.question: Which classic Disneyland dark ride takes guests on a wild drive with Mr. Toad?
questions.explanation: Mr. Toad's Wild Ride is a dark ride in Fantasyland based on The Wind in the Willows character.

questions.question: Space Mountain at Disneyland Park is located in which land?
questions.explanation: Space Mountain is an indoor space-themed coaster located in Disneyland's Tomorrowland.

questions.question: Radiator Springs Racers is the marquee attraction of which Disney California Adventure land?
questions.explanation: Radiator Springs Racers is a racing dark ride located in Cars Land at Disney California Adventure.

questions.question: Guardians of the Galaxy - Mission: BREAKOUT! took over the building formerly home to which ride?
questions.explanation: Guardians of the Galaxy - Mission: BREAKOUT! took over the building formerly home to Twilight Zone Tower of Terror.
questions.sourceTitle: Guardians of the Galaxy - Mission: BREAKOUT!

questions.question: Which Disney California Adventure raft ride splashes through a simulated national park river?
questions.explanation: Grizzly River Run is a whitewater raft ride located in the Grizzly Peak area of the park.

questions.question: What nighttime spectacular is performed on Paradise Bay at Disney California Adventure?
questions.explanation: World of Color is a nighttime water and light show performed on Paradise Bay at Disney California Adventure.

questions.question: Which land at Disney California Adventure is themed to a seaside boardwalk with a Pixar twist?
questions.explanation: Pixar Pier reimagines a classic seaside amusement pier with Pixar film theming.

questions.question: Which Cars Land attraction is themed to tractor tipping?
questions.explanation: Mater's Junkyard Jamboree is a spinning ride in Cars Land themed to Mater's tractor-tipping antics.

questions.question: The Soarin' attraction debuted at which park before spreading to others?
questions.explanation: Soarin' Over California opened first at Disney California Adventure before versions appeared at other parks.

questions.question: Which land at Disney California Adventure celebrates classic movie studio backlots?
questions.explanation: Hollywood Land is themed to the golden age of movie studio backlots at Disney California Adventure.

questions.question: Luigi's Rollickin' Roadsters is themed around characters from which Pixar film?
questions.explanation: Luigi's Rollickin' Roadsters in Cars Land is themed to Luigi's tire shop from the Cars films.

questions.question: Which was the first Disney theme park built outside the United States?
questions.explanation: Tokyo Disneyland opened in 1983, becoming the first Disney park built outside the United States.

questions.question: Which Disney park is themed entirely around nautical exploration and the sea, unique among Disney resorts?
questions.explanation: Tokyo DisneySea is a one-of-a-kind park built around seafaring myths and exploration themes.

questions.question: Which Disney resort sits on reclaimed land near Tokyo Bay in Japan?
questions.explanation: Tokyo Disney Resort was built on reclaimed land in Urayasu, near Tokyo Bay.

questions.question: Which Disney park features the Enchanted Storybook Castle, the tallest of any Disney castle?
questions.explanation: Shanghai Disneyland's Enchanted Storybook Castle is the tallest and largest castle at any Disney park.

questions.question: Hong Kong Disneyland is located on which island?
questions.explanation: Hong Kong Disneyland is located on Lantau Island within the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region.

questions.question: What is the French name given to Sleeping Beauty Castle at Disneyland Paris?
answers: Le Chateau de la Belle au Bois Dormant
questions.explanation: Disneyland Paris's castle is officially named for Sleeping Beauty, La Belle au Bois Dormant in French.

questions.question: Which international Disney park opened most recently, in 2016?
questions.explanation: Shanghai Disneyland Resort opened in June 2016, the newest of the international Disney parks.

questions.question: Space Mountain: Mission 2 is a reimagined version of the classic coaster found at which park?
questions.explanation: Space Mountain: Mission 2 is a reimagined version of the classic coaster located at Disneyland Paris.

questions.question: Which Tokyo Disney Resort park closely mirrors Magic Kingdom's layout, including a Fantasyland and Westernland?
questions.explanation: Tokyo Disneyland features classic lands like Fantasyland, closely modeled on Magic Kingdom's layout.

questions.question: What type of ride is "it's a small world," found at multiple Disney parks worldwide?
questions.explanation: "it's a small world" is a slow-moving boat ride featuring audio-animatronic children from around the globe.

questions.question: What type of ride vehicle do guests use on Big Thunder Mountain Railroad?
questions.explanation: Big Thunder Mountain Railroad uses runaway mine train vehicles themed to an old mining operation.

questions.question: On the Jungle Cruise, guests travel by which kind of vehicle?
questions.explanation: The Jungle Cruise uses tour boats guided by a skipper who narrates a comedic river voyage.

questions.question: Which attraction uses continuously moving "Doom Buggy" vehicles to carry guests past ghostly scenes?
questions.explanation: The Haunted Mansion uses Doom Buggy ride vehicles to move guests continuously through its scenes.

questions.question: Which attraction is themed to a boat voyage past pirates raiding a Caribbean town?
questions.explanation: Pirates of the Caribbean is a boat ride depicting swashbuckling pirates raiding a Caribbean seaport.

questions.question: Which attraction lets guests pilot a simulated starfighter through a Star Wars adventure?
questions.explanation: Star Tours - The Adventures Continue is a motion-simulator ride themed to Star Wars starfighter journeys.

questions.question: Which classic attraction spins guests around in oversized teacups?
questions.explanation: The Mad Tea Party spins guests in oversized teacups inspired by Alice in Wonderland.

questions.question: Which attraction uses rocket-shaped vehicles that circle a central tower in Tomorrowland?
questions.explanation: Astro Orbiter is a spinning ride with rocket vehicles that circle a central tower in Tomorrowland.

questions.question: Which attraction lets guests drive their own small gasoline-powered cars along a track?
questions.explanation: Autopia at Disneyland lets guests drive gasoline-powered cars along a winding track.

questions.question: Which Magic Kingdom land is themed to the colonial-era United States?
questions.explanation: Liberty Square is themed to colonial and revolutionary-era America at Magic Kingdom.

questions.question: Which land recreates a turn-of-the-century American town at the entrance of Magic Kingdom?
questions.explanation: Main Street, U.S.A. greets guests at the entrance of Magic Kingdom with early 1900s small-town theming.

questions.question: Which land at Magic Kingdom is themed to the American frontier and Wild West?
questions.explanation: Frontierland recreates the rugged American Old West at Magic Kingdom.

questions.question: Cars Land is a themed land found at which Disney park?
questions.explanation: Cars Land, themed to the Pixar Cars films, is located at Disney California Adventure.

questions.question: Which EPCOT area contains World Showcase pavilions representing eleven countries?
questions.explanation: World Showcase is the section of EPCOT featuring pavilions representing eleven countries around a lagoon.

questions.question: Toy Story Land, which shrinks guests to the size of a toy, is located at which park?
questions.explanation: Toy Story Land at Hollywood Studios places guests in Andy's toy-sized backyard.

questions.question: Which Animal Kingdom land is themed to a fictional bioluminescent alien moon?
questions.explanation: Pandora - The World of Avatar is themed to the glowing alien moon from the film Avatar.

questions.question: Critter Country at Disneyland Park is themed to which type of setting?
questions.explanation: Critter Country at Disneyland is themed to a rustic, woodland trapper's outpost setting.

questions.question: Which land at Hollywood Studios recreates a bustling movie studio street from the 1930s?
questions.explanation: Hollywood Boulevard recreates a glamorous 1930s Hollywood street at the entrance of Hollywood Studios.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World resort hotel is themed to a grand Victorian seaside retreat?
questions.explanation: Disney's Grand Floridian Resort & Spa is themed to an elegant Victorian-era beach resort.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World resort features rooms inside an A-frame tower with the monorail passing through it?
questions.explanation: Disney's Contemporary Resort is built as a large A-frame tower with the monorail passing through it.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World resort is themed to a rustic national park lodge?
questions.explanation: Disney's Wilderness Lodge draws inspiration from grand national park lodges of the American West.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World resort lets guests view African-inspired savanna wildlife from their rooms?
questions.explanation: Disney's Animal Kingdom Lodge features savanna views where guests can spot live African animals.

questions.question: Which Disneyland Resort hotel connects directly into Disney California Adventure via a walkway?
questions.explanation: Disney's Grand Californian Hotel & Spa has a direct entrance connecting into Disney California Adventure.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World resort hotel is themed to the islands of the South Pacific?
questions.explanation: Disney's Polynesian Village Resort is themed to the islands of the South Pacific.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World resorts are themed to a nautical New England harbor town from the 1880s?
answers: Disney's Yacht Club and Beach Club Resorts
questions.explanation: Disney's Yacht Club and Beach Club Resorts are themed to a nautical New England harbor from the 1880s.

questions.question: What is the original flagship hotel of the Disneyland Resort, opened in 1955?
questions.explanation: The Disneyland Hotel opened in 1955 as the original resort hotel serving Disneyland Park guests.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World resort recreates a lively French Quarter and Louisiana bayou setting?
questions.explanation: Disney's Port Orleans Resort is themed to New Orleans' French Quarter and the Louisiana bayou.

questions.question: What type of transportation connects Magic Kingdom, EPCOT, and several Walt Disney World resorts overhead?
questions.explanation: The Walt Disney World Monorail system links Magic Kingdom, EPCOT, and nearby resort hotels.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World transportation system uses gondola cabins suspended from cables?
questions.explanation: The Disney Skyliner is an aerial gondola system connecting Hollywood Studios, EPCOT, and several resorts.

questions.question: Besides the monorail, which mode of transportation carries guests across the Seven Seas Lagoon to Magic Kingdom?
questions.explanation: A ferryboat carries guests across the Seven Seas Lagoon from the Transportation and Ticket Center to Magic Kingdom.

questions.question: What is the name of the slow-moving people-mover attraction in Magic Kingdom's Tomorrowland?
questions.explanation: The Tomorrowland Transit Authority PeopleMover carries guests on a leisurely tour above Tomorrowland.

questions.question: Which vintage-style transportation vehicles carry guests along Main Street, U.S.A.?
questions.explanation: Double-decker omnibuses are among the vintage vehicles that carry guests along Main Street, U.S.A.

questions.question: What type of boats shuttle guests between EPCOT's World Showcase and nearby resort hotels?
questions.explanation: Friendship boats carry guests across World Showcase Lagoon between EPCOT and nearby resorts.

questions.question: Which railroad circles the perimeter of Disneyland Park, stopping at several themed lands?
questions.explanation: The Disneyland Railroad circles the park's perimeter, stopping at several themed lands.

questions.question: In what year did Disney's Magical Express airport shuttle service end?
questions.explanation: Disney's Magical Express provided complimentary transportation between Orlando International Airport and Disney resorts until it ended in January 2022.

questions.question: Which Skyliner station provides direct gondola access to Disney's Hollywood Studios?
questions.explanation: The Disney Skyliner includes a dedicated station providing direct access to Disney's Hollywood Studios.

questions.question: Fantasmic! is a long-running nighttime show performed at Disneyland Park and which other Disney park?
questions.explanation: Fantasmic! is a nighttime show performed at both Disneyland Park and Disney's Hollywood Studios.

questions.question: Festival of the Lion King is a live stage show featuring puppetry and acrobatics at which park?
questions.explanation: Festival of the Lion King is a live stage show combining puppetry, acrobatics, and music at Disney's Animal Kingdom.

questions.question: Which retired EPCOT nighttime show combined fireworks and lasers around World Showcase Lagoon?
questions.explanation: Illuminations: Reflections of Earth was a long-running fireworks and laser show performed on World Showcase Lagoon.

questions.question: The Main Street Electrical Parade first debuted in 1972 at which Disney park?
questions.explanation: The Main Street Electrical Parade debuted at Disneyland Park in 1972, lit by thousands of small bulbs.

questions.question: Which retired Magic Kingdom nighttime parade eventually succeeded the Main Street Electrical Parade there?
questions.explanation: SpectroMagic was a retired nighttime parade that succeeded the Main Street Electrical Parade at Magic Kingdom.

questions.question: The Festival of Fantasy Parade at Magic Kingdom features a large fire-breathing float themed to which villain?
questions.explanation: The Festival of Fantasy Parade includes a towering Maleficent dragon float that breathes fire.

questions.question: Which all-LED nighttime parade debuted at Disneyland Park during its Diamond Celebration in 2015?
questions.explanation: Paint the Night was an all-LED parade that debuted at Disneyland Park for its 60th anniversary celebration.

questions.question: Voices of Liberty, an a cappella singing group, regularly performs in which EPCOT pavilion?
questions.explanation: Voices of Liberty is an a cappella group that performs patriotic songs in EPCOT's American Adventure pavilion.

questions.question: Jedi Training is an interactive stage show found in Star Wars-themed lands, where guests do what?
questions.explanation: Jedi Training is a stage show where young guests learn to duel with lightsabers against Star Wars villains.

questions.question: In what year did Walt Disney World's Magic Kingdom officially open to the public?
questions.explanation: Magic Kingdom opened on October 1, 1971, as the first park at Walt Disney World.

questions.question: In what year did the original Disneyland Park open in Anaheim, California?
questions.explanation: Disneyland Park opened on July 17, 1955, in Anaheim, California.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World park opened in 1982 as the resort's second theme park?
questions.explanation: EPCOT opened October 1, 1982, becoming the second theme park at Walt Disney World.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World park opened in 1989, focused on movies and television production?
questions.explanation: The park now known as Disney's Hollywood Studios opened in 1989 as Disney-MGM Studios.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World park, opened in 1998, is dedicated to wildlife and conservation?
questions.explanation: Disney's Animal Kingdom opened in April 1998, focused on wildlife, conservation, and adventure.

questions.question: What was the original name of Disney's Hollywood Studios when it first opened?
questions.explanation: Disney's Hollywood Studios opened under the name Disney-MGM Studios before its later rebranding.

questions.question: Which Disneyland Resort park opened in 2001 as a companion to Disneyland Park?
questions.explanation: Disney California Adventure opened in February 2001 next to the original Disneyland Park.

questions.question: EPCOT's name was originally derived from what concept championed by Walt Disney?
answers: Enchanted Park of Creative Original Themes
Educational Program for Cultural Outreach Today
questions.explanation: EPCOT stands for Experimental Prototype Community of Tomorrow, a concept originally envisioned by Walt Disney.

questions.question: Which was the first Disney park to open in Europe?
questions.explanation: Disneyland Paris opened in 1992, becoming the first Disney park in Europe.

questions.question: Which retired Magic Kingdom attraction sent guests on a log flume ride through Br'er Rabbit's briar patch?
questions.explanation: Splash Mountain was a log flume ride themed to Br'er Rabbit folk tales before its retirement and retheming.

questions.question: Which retired EPCOT attraction took guests through visions of future homes and communities?
questions.explanation: Horizons was a retired EPCOT attraction that toured guests through visions of future homes and communities.

questions.question: Which retired Hollywood Studios attraction took guests through re-created scenes from classic films?
questions.explanation: The Great Movie Ride took guests through re-created scenes from classic films before it closed in 2017.

questions.question: Which retired Tomorrowland attraction starred a time-traveling robot host named Timekeeper?
questions.explanation: The Timekeeper was a circle-vision film attraction hosted by a robot character exploring time travel.

questions.question: Which retired Magic Kingdom attraction took guests on a submarine voyage inspired by a Jules Verne novel?
answers: 20,000 Leagues Under the Sea: Submarine Voyage
questions.explanation: 20,000 Leagues Under the Sea: Submarine Voyage was a retired Magic Kingdom ride based on the Jules Verne novel.
questions.sourceTitle: 20,000 Leagues Under the Sea: Submarine Voyage

questions.question: Which retired EPCOT attraction was a simulator ride that sent guests journeying through the human body?
questions.explanation: Body Wars was a simulator ride in EPCOT's retired Wonders of Life pavilion, sending guests through the human body.

questions.question: Which retired Disneyland Tomorrowland attraction simulated a rocket trip to the moon?
questions.explanation: Rocket to the Moon was an early Disneyland Tomorrowland attraction simulating a lunar space flight.

questions.question: Which retired Magic Kingdom dark ride told a frightening version of Snow White's story before its 2021 reimagining?
questions.explanation: Snow White's Scary Adventures was a Fantasyland dark ride retired and replaced by Snow White's Enchanted Wish.

questions.question: Which Magic Kingdom Adventureland show features Audio-Animatronic birds singing tropical songs?
questions.explanation: Walt Disney's Enchanted Tiki Room in Adventureland stars singing Audio-Animatronic parrots and other tropical birds.

questions.question: The Prince Charming Regal Carrousel spins guests in which Magic Kingdom land?
questions.explanation: The Prince Charming Regal Carrousel is a classic carousel located in Magic Kingdom's Fantasyland.

questions.question: Which patriotic Magic Kingdom attraction features Audio-Animatronic figures of every U.S. president?
questions.explanation: The Hall of Presidents in Liberty Square presents Audio-Animatronic figures of all U.S. presidents.

questions.question: The Country Bear Jamboree, a musical revue with singing bears, sits in which Magic Kingdom land?
questions.explanation: The Country Bear Jamboree is a stage show of Audio-Animatronic bears located in Magic Kingdom's Frontierland.

questions.question: The main Walt Disney World Railroad station sits above the entrance to which Magic Kingdom land?
questions.explanation: The Walt Disney World Railroad's main depot stands above the entrance to Main Street, U.S.A. at Magic Kingdom.

questions.question: Which Magic Kingdom Adventureland spinner ride is themed to Disney's Aladdin?
questions.explanation: The Magic Carpets of Aladdin is an Adventureland spinner where guests ride carpets around a genie's bottle.

questions.question: Which Magic Kingdom junior coaster casts Goofy as a barnstorming stunt pilot?
questions.explanation: The Barnstormer is a small family coaster in Storybook Circus themed to Goofy performing aerial stunts.

questions.question: Which Magic Kingdom Tomorrowland attraction lets guests drive gasoline-powered cars along a track?
questions.explanation: The Tomorrowland Speedway lets Magic Kingdom guests steer gas-powered cars along a guided racetrack.

questions.question: Which log-flume attraction replaced Splash Mountain at Walt Disney World's Magic Kingdom in 2024?
questions.explanation: Tiana's Bayou Adventure, based on The Princess and the Frog, replaced Splash Mountain at Magic Kingdom in 2024.

questions.question: The Spaceship Earth attraction at EPCOT takes guests on a journey through the history of what?
questions.explanation: Spaceship Earth, inside EPCOT's geodesic sphere, traces the history of human communication.

questions.question: Which EPCOT World Showcase pavilion features a scaled replica of the Eiffel Tower?
questions.explanation: EPCOT's France pavilion includes a forced-perspective replica of the Eiffel Tower.

questions.question: Which automobile brand presents EPCOT's Test Track attraction?
questions.explanation: Test Track at EPCOT is presented by Chevrolet, letting guests design and test a virtual vehicle.

questions.question: Which EPCOT attraction simulates astronaut training and a launch toward Mars?
questions.explanation: Mission: SPACE is an EPCOT centrifuge simulator sending guests on a mission toward Mars.

questions.question: Which EPCOT roller coaster is themed to the Guardians of the Galaxy?
answers: Guardians of the Galaxy: Cosmic Rewind
questions.explanation: Guardians of the Galaxy: Cosmic Rewind is an indoor coaster at EPCOT that opened in 2022.
questions.sourceTitle: Guardians of the Galaxy: Cosmic Rewind

questions.question: Which EPCOT World Showcase pavilion features a replica of St. Mark's Campanile bell tower?
questions.explanation: EPCOT's Italy pavilion includes a replica of the Campanile bell tower from Venice's St. Mark's Square.

questions.question: Which EPCOT attraction stars Figment, a small purple dragon?
questions.explanation: Journey Into Imagination with Figment features the purple dragon Figment exploring the senses.

questions.question: Which EPCOT World Showcase pavilion features a glockenspiel and a miniature model train village?
questions.explanation: EPCOT's Germany pavilion has a glockenspiel clock tower and a detailed miniature railway village.

questions.question: Which Hollywood Studios trackless dark ride is themed to a Mickey Mouse cartoon short?
questions.explanation: Mickey & Minnie's Runaway Railway is a trackless dark ride based on the Mickey Mouse cartoon shorts.

questions.question: On which Hollywood Studios attraction do guests fire at 3D carnival targets with Toy Story characters?
questions.explanation: Toy Story Mania! is a 4D interactive shooting ride through midway games hosted by Toy Story characters.

questions.question: The Twilight Zone Tower of Terror at Hollywood Studios is set in which fictional hotel?
questions.explanation: Hollywood Studios' Tower of Terror is set in the haunted, run-down Hollywood Tower Hotel.
questions.sourceTitle: The Twilight Zone Tower of Terror

questions.question: Which full-scale replica building serves as an icon at the end of Hollywood Boulevard at Hollywood Studios?
questions.explanation: A replica of Grauman's Chinese Theatre anchors Hollywood Boulevard at Disney's Hollywood Studios.

questions.question: What themed cantina in Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge serves drinks to guests?
questions.explanation: Oga's Cantina is the Star Wars-themed bar inside Galaxy's Edge at Disney's Hollywood Studios.

questions.question: In the story of Hollywood Studios' Slinky Dog Dash, who is imagined to have built the coaster?
questions.explanation: Slinky Dog Dash is themed as a coaster the character Andy built from a Mega Coaster toy kit.

questions.question: Alien Swirling Saucers at Hollywood Studios is themed to a toy set won from which pizza restaurant?
questions.explanation: Alien Swirling Saucers is styled as a Pizza Planet toy set, spinning guests in rockets with the aliens.

questions.question: Which Hollywood Studios restaurant seats guests in convertible cars at a drive-in movie?
questions.explanation: The Sci-Fi Dine-In Theater Restaurant seats diners in car-shaped booths facing sci-fi movie clips.

questions.question: Which Hollywood Studios live show recreates action and stunts from the Indiana Jones films?
answers: Beauty and the Beast - Live on Stage
questions.explanation: The Indiana Jones Epic Stunt Spectacular restages elaborate stunt scenes from Raiders of the Lost Ark.

questions.question: The Tree of Life, Animal Kingdom's icon, is famous for its trunk carved with images of what?
questions.explanation: Animal Kingdom's Tree of Life is sculpted with hundreds of intertwined animal carvings.

questions.question: On Animal Kingdom's DINOSAUR ride, guests race to rescue which dinosaur before a meteor strike?
questions.explanation: DINOSAUR sends guests back in time to bring an Iguanodon named Aladar forward before a meteor hits.

questions.question: Which creature serves as the guardian that menaces trains on Animal Kingdom's Expedition Everest?
questions.explanation: Expedition Everest is guarded by a large Audio-Animatronic Yeti protecting the Forbidden Mountain.

questions.question: On Avatar Flight of Passage at Animal Kingdom, guests soar on the back of which creature?
questions.explanation: Avatar Flight of Passage lets guests fly on a mountain banshee, called an ikran, over Pandora.

questions.question: Which Animal Kingdom train carries guests from the Africa land to Rafiki's Planet Watch?
questions.explanation: The Wildlife Express Train transports guests from Harambe to the Rafiki's Planet Watch conservation area.

questions.question: The Maharajah Jungle Trek, a walking trail with tigers, is found in which Animal Kingdom land?
questions.explanation: The Maharajah Jungle Trek is a self-guided animal trail featuring tigers in Animal Kingdom's Asia.

questions.question: Which self-guided Animal Kingdom trail lets guests view gorillas and hippos in the Africa land?
questions.explanation: The Gorilla Falls Exploration Trail winds through the Africa land past gorillas, hippos, and birds.

questions.question: Which spinning ride in Animal Kingdom's DinoLand U.S.A. is themed to a dinosaur?
questions.explanation: TriceraTop Spin is a dinosaur-themed hub-and-spoke spinner in Animal Kingdom's DinoLand U.S.A.

questions.question: What is the name of Animal Kingdom's lush entrance land of gardens and animal exhibits?
questions.explanation: The Oasis is the tropical garden land guests pass through when entering Disney's Animal Kingdom.

questions.question: Which Disneyland dark ride follows the story of a wooden puppet who wants to be a real boy?
questions.explanation: Pinocchio's Daring Journey is a Fantasyland dark ride at Disneyland retelling Pinocchio's story.

questions.question: Which Disneyland Fantasyland dark ride sends guests down a rabbit hole in caterpillar vehicles?
questions.explanation: Disneyland's Alice in Wonderland dark ride carries guests in caterpillar cars through Wonderland.

questions.question: The Storybook Land Canal Boats at Disneyland enter their miniature world through the mouth of whom?
questions.explanation: Disneyland's Storybook Land Canal Boats begin by sailing through the open mouth of Monstro the whale.

questions.question: What is the name of the full-scale sailing ship that circles the Rivers of America at Disneyland?
questions.explanation: The Sailing Ship Columbia is a replica three-masted vessel that circles Disneyland's Rivers of America.

questions.question: What kind of vessel is the Mark Twain Riverboat at Disneyland?
questions.explanation: The Mark Twain Riverboat is a steam-powered paddlewheel riverboat circling Disneyland's Rivers of America.

questions.question: Which Disneyland Tomorrowland submarine ride is themed to a Pixar film about a lost clownfish?
questions.explanation: The Finding Nemo Submarine Voyage sends Disneyland guests underwater to search for Nemo.

questions.question: What is the name of the classic carousel at the center of Disneyland's Fantasyland?
questions.explanation: The King Arthur Carrousel is the antique carousel standing behind Sleeping Beauty Castle at Disneyland.

questions.question: The Disneyland Railroad passes a diorama of dinosaurs known by what name?
questions.explanation: The Disneyland Railroad travels through the Primeval World diorama depicting dinosaurs.

questions.question: What 1920s-style theater serves as the icon of Disney California Adventure?
questions.explanation: A replica of the Carthay Circle Theatre stands as the centerpiece icon of Disney California Adventure.

questions.question: What is the name of the Mickey-faced Ferris wheel at Disney California Adventure's Pixar Pier?
questions.explanation: Pixar Pal-A-Round is the giant Ferris wheel with both stationary and swinging gondolas at Pixar Pier.

questions.question: The Incredicoaster at Disney California Adventure is themed to which Pixar superhero family?
questions.explanation: The Incredicoaster at Pixar Pier is a launch coaster themed to Pixar's The Incredibles.

questions.question: Buena Vista Street, the entrance land at Disney California Adventure, recreates Los Angeles of which era?
questions.explanation: Buena Vista Street evokes 1920s Los Angeles, the era when Walt Disney arrived in California.

questions.question: Grizzly River Run, a raft ride, is located in which Disney California Adventure land?
questions.explanation: Grizzly River Run splashes through the wilderness-themed Grizzly Peak land at Disney California Adventure.

questions.question: Which Disney California Adventure wild-mouse coaster is themed to a Goofy how-to cartoon?
questions.explanation: Goofy's Sky School is a wild-mouse coaster styled after a Goofy flight-instruction cartoon.

questions.question: What retro rocket ride swings guests outward on Disney California Adventure's Paradise Gardens?
questions.explanation: The Golden Zephyr swings rocket ships outward on cables, evoking 1920s science-fiction serials.

questions.question: Which Disney California Adventure dark ride retells Ariel's story in clamshell vehicles?
answers: Under the Sea: Journey of the Little Mermaid
questions.explanation: The Little Mermaid ~ Ariel's Undersea Adventure carries guests through The Little Mermaid in clamshells.
questions.sourceTitle: The Little Mermaid ~ Ariel's Undersea Adventure

questions.question: Cars Land's main street at Disney California Adventure is themed after which famous highway?
questions.explanation: Cars Land recreates Radiator Springs along a version of the historic Route 66 from the film Cars.

questions.question: Which Tokyo DisneySea port of call features a giant volcano called Mount Prometheus?
questions.explanation: Tokyo DisneySea's Mysterious Island sits inside the fictional volcano Mount Prometheus.

questions.question: Which Tokyo DisneySea trackless dark ride is themed to the voyages of Sinbad the sailor?
answers: Journey to the Center of the Earth
questions.explanation: Sindbad's Storybook Voyage is a gentle boat ride at Tokyo DisneySea following Sinbad's adventures.

questions.question: Which unique trackless dark ride sits in Hong Kong Disneyland's Mystic Point land?
questions.explanation: Mystic Manor is a trackless dark ride exclusive to Hong Kong Disneyland's Mystic Point.

questions.question: Which Disneyland Paris theme park is dedicated to film and hosts Ratatouille: The Adventure?
questions.explanation: Walt Disney Studios Park is the movie-themed second gate at the Disneyland Paris resort.

questions.question: At Disneyland Paris, the Haunted Mansion is reimagined as a Western ghost story called what?
questions.explanation: Phantom Manor is Disneyland Paris's darker, Old West version of the Haunted Mansion.

questions.question: What is the Jules Verne-inspired futuristic land at Disneyland Paris called?
questions.explanation: Disneyland Paris replaces Tomorrowland with Discoveryland, themed to Jules Verne and retro-futurism.

questions.question: Which Shanghai Disneyland land is themed to pirates and hosts Battle for the Sunken Treasure?
questions.explanation: Treasure Cove is Shanghai Disneyland's pirate-themed land, home to Pirates of the Caribbean: Battle for the Sunken Treasure.

questions.question: Which Tokyo Disneyland trackless ride uses hunny-pot vehicles to follow Winnie the Pooh?
answers: The Many Adventures of Winnie the Pooh
questions.explanation: Pooh's Hunny Hunt is a pioneering trackless dark ride at Tokyo Disneyland.

questions.question: Which Japanese company owns and operates the Tokyo Disney Resort under license from Disney?
questions.explanation: The Oriental Land Company owns and runs Tokyo Disney Resort under a license from The Walt Disney Company.

questions.question: Jungle Cruise skippers are known for narrating the boat tour with what?
questions.explanation: Jungle Cruise skippers deliver a comedic script full of groan-worthy puns during the boat tour.

questions.question: Space Mountain is an indoor roller coaster set in what kind of environment?
questions.explanation: Space Mountain sends riders through the dark on a coaster themed to a journey through outer space.

questions.question: Big Thunder Mountain Railroad is a runaway mine train set in what kind of landscape?
questions.explanation: Big Thunder Mountain Railroad races through a Southwestern desert mining region on a runaway train.

questions.question: Which classic attraction features the theme song 'Grim Grinning Ghosts'?
questions.explanation: 'Grim Grinning Ghosts' is the signature theme song of Disney's Haunted Mansion attraction.

questions.question: The Pirates of the Caribbean attraction inspired a film series centered on which character?
questions.explanation: The Pirates of the Caribbean ride inspired the film franchise led by Captain Jack Sparrow.

questions.question: Which classic attraction features the song 'There's a Great Big Beautiful Tomorrow'?
questions.explanation: Walt Disney's Carousel of Progress uses the song 'There's a Great Big Beautiful Tomorrow' as its theme.

questions.question: Which parrot host opens the show in Walt Disney's Enchanted Tiki Room?
questions.explanation: Jose is the emcee parrot who greets guests and leads the birds in the Enchanted Tiki Room.

questions.question: The Mad Tea Party spinning teacup ride is based on which Disney animated film?
questions.explanation: The Mad Tea Party spins guests in teacups themed to the unbirthday party in Alice in Wonderland.

questions.question: Besides wind and motion, Soarin' Around the World immerses riders with what sensory effect?
questions.explanation: Soarin' Around the World adds scents like grass and sea air to its hang-gliding simulation.

questions.question: New Orleans Square at Disneyland is home to Pirates of the Caribbean and which other classic attraction?
questions.explanation: Disneyland's New Orleans Square contains both Pirates of the Caribbean and the Haunted Mansion.

questions.question: Fantasyland at Magic Kingdom is located directly behind which landmark?
questions.explanation: Magic Kingdom's Fantasyland sits directly behind Cinderella Castle at the center of the park.

questions.question: What is the name of the frontier village setting of Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge?
questions.explanation: Galaxy's Edge is set in Black Spire Outpost, a trading village on the planet Batuu.

questions.question: In which year did the cartoon land Mickey's Toontown first open at Disneyland?
questions.explanation: Mickey's Toontown opened at Disneyland in 1993 as a walkable cartoon town for Mickey and friends.

questions.question: The floating mountains in Pandora at Animal Kingdom are known by what name from the film Avatar?
questions.explanation: Pandora at Animal Kingdom recreates the floating Hallelujah Mountains from the film Avatar.

questions.question: Cars Land at Disney California Adventure recreates which town from the Pixar film Cars?
questions.explanation: Cars Land is a full-scale recreation of Radiator Springs, the town from Pixar's Cars.

questions.question: Which Tokyo DisneySea land is themed to a mermaid's underwater kingdom?
questions.explanation: Mermaid Lagoon at Tokyo DisneySea is themed to the undersea world of The Little Mermaid.

questions.question: Which Hong Kong Disneyland land immerses guests in Andy's backyard from Toy Story?
questions.explanation: Hong Kong Disneyland's Toy Story Land shrinks guests to toy size in Andy's backyard.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World value resort is themed to decades of 20th-century American pop culture?
questions.explanation: Disney's Pop Century Resort at Walt Disney World is themed to fads and fashions of past decades.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World resort has wings themed to Cars, Finding Nemo, and The Lion King?
questions.explanation: Disney's Art of Animation Resort features buildings themed to Cars, Finding Nemo, The Lion King, and The Little Mermaid.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World deluxe resort is themed to an early-1900s Atlantic seaside boardwalk?
questions.explanation: Disney's BoardWalk Inn recreates a 1900s Atlantic City-style boardwalk near EPCOT.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World resort is themed to the American Southwest and features a Mayan pyramid pool?
questions.explanation: Disney's Coronado Springs Resort is themed to the American Southwest and Mexico, with a Mayan pyramid pool.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World resort, opened in 2019, is themed to the European Riviera?
questions.explanation: Disney's Riviera Resort, opened in 2019, evokes the Mediterranean coast of the European Riviera.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World moderate resort is themed to colorful villages of the Caribbean islands?
questions.explanation: Disney's Caribbean Beach Resort surrounds a lake with buildings themed to different Caribbean islands.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World resort offers a woodsy campground with cabins near Bay Lake?
questions.explanation: Disney's Fort Wilderness Resort & Campground offers rustic campsites and cabins in a wooded setting.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World resort, themed to the Florida Keys, was Disney's first Vacation Club property?
questions.explanation: Disney's Old Key West Resort, themed to the Florida Keys, opened in 1991 as the first Disney Vacation Club resort.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World resort is themed to an 1880s upstate New York horse-racing retreat?
questions.explanation: Disney's Saratoga Springs Resort & Spa evokes an 1880s Saratoga Springs, New York spa and racing town.

questions.question: Which resort serves as the central transfer hub of the Disney Skyliner gondola system?
questions.explanation: The Disney Skyliner's lines converge at a hub station beside Disney's Caribbean Beach Resort.

questions.question: Which retired overhead gondola ride carried Disneyland guests between Fantasyland and Tomorrowland?
questions.explanation: The Skyway was an aerial gondola ride linking Fantasyland and Tomorrowland at Disneyland until 1994.

questions.question: The Tomorrowland Transit Authority PeopleMover at Magic Kingdom moves using what kind of motor?
questions.explanation: The PeopleMover glides on linear induction motors with no onboard engine or emissions.

questions.question: Which Disneyland Main Street vehicle is pulled by a horse down Main Street, U.S.A.?
questions.explanation: The Horse-Drawn Streetcar is a horse-pulled trolley among Disneyland's Main Street Vehicles.

questions.question: In which decade did the Disneyland Monorail open as the first daily monorail in the Americas?
questions.explanation: The Disneyland Monorail opened in 1959, becoming the first daily-operating monorail in the Western Hemisphere.

questions.question: The Walt Disney World Railroad circles Magic Kingdom pulled by what kind of restored engines?
questions.explanation: The Walt Disney World Railroad uses restored antique steam locomotives to circle Magic Kingdom.

questions.question: What 'Mark' designation identifies the current fleet of Walt Disney World Monorail trains?
questions.explanation: The Walt Disney World Monorail currently operates the Mark VI generation of trains.

questions.question: The Liberty Belle riverboat at Magic Kingdom travels around which body of water?
